races.race_name: Clive Cox Racing EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive) (Div I)

runners.name: Benefacta
runners.spotlight: Inexperience evident during the first half of last month's debut outing at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) but the penny dropped somewhat and he finished seventh of 12, beaten just over 3l at 11-1; this half-brother to three winners may have learnt a lot from that initial experience.
runners.comment: Green on debut but showed promise; may have learnt a lot from that initial experience

runners.name: Minzazi
runners.spotlight: 42,000gns foal, 58,000gns yearling; sixth foal; Minzaal half-brother to winners Another Investment (6f/7f including 2yo; RPR 95), Shrara (6f 2yo; 71) and Ibn Alwaleed (KSA 1m 2yo); dam US 5f 2yo/6f dirt winner; stable has made a strong start to June and the betting could be informative on debut.
runners.comment: Stable has made a strong start to June and the betting could be informative on debut

runners.name: Prince Kameo
runners.spotlight: He's shown ability on both starts (6f, AW/good) but not enough to suggest he'll be coming out on top today; nurseries beckon.
runners.comment: He's shown ability on both runs but not enough to suggest he'll be coming out on top today

runners.name: Glory Bound
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros yearling, resold 17,000gns yearling; first foal; dam 7f AW 2yo winner (RPR 84), half-sister to 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Leovanni; this Cotai Glory newcomer may improve for the run.
runners.comment: 20,000euros yearling, resold 17,000gns yearling; this newcomer may improve for the run

runners.name: Ten Year Stretch
runners.spotlight: 11,000euros yearling; fourth foal; Inns Of Court half-brother to Italian 7.5f 2yo-1m2f winner Nuncepossocrede; dam unplaced 1m-11.4f (RPR 67), half-sister to three winners; trainer hasn't run many 2yos this year but there have been promising signs; could run well on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer hasn't run many 2yos this year but there have been promising signs; could run well

runners.name: Undiscovered
runners.spotlight: 55,000gns yearling; second foal; dam runner-up in France, half-sister to winners Al Wukair (1m Group 1) and Dream Today (useful 6f/7f), out of 1m 2yo winner; top trainer has solid 23% strike-rate with 2yos here in the last five seasons; this New Bay debutant is one to be interested in.
runners.comment: 55,000gns yearling; dam half-sister to Group 1 winner Al Wukair; one to be interested in

runners.name: Lord Of Winterfell
runners.spotlight: 30,000euros yearling, 32,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; second foal; dam 7f/1m winner (including 2yo/US; RPR 96), half-sister to 1m4f/1m6f winner Codiak, out of French Listed-placed 1m4f winner; a watching brief may prove wisest with this New Bay newcomer.
runners.comment: 32,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; a watching brief may prove wisest with this New Bay newcomer

runners.name: Foscarini
runners.spotlight: 9,000euros foal, 45,000gns yearling; first foal; dam placed 7f AW (RPR 71), half-sister to winners Double March (7f 2yo/1m2f) and Emperor's Son (5f including 2yo), out of unraced half-sister to 1m Listed winner; this Without Parole newcomer could go well for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: 45,000gns yearling; yard in good form and this newcomer could go well

runners.name: Half A Hoof
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to four winners and a market check is advised on this second start, but she was well beaten at 18-1 in a fillies' novice at Ascot (5f, good) on last month's debut.
runners.comment: Half-sister to four winners but well beaten at 18-1 in a fillies' novice at Ascot on debut

runners.name: Secret Society
runners.spotlight: 20,000euros foal, £48,000 yearling; eighth foal; Cotai Glory brother to two winners including Time Douglas (Italian 5f/6f), half-brother to 7f/1m winner Linwood (including 2yo; RPR 116); trainer sponsors the race and has enjoyed plenty of success in it in recent years; has to be respected on debut.
runners.comment: Trainer sponsors the race and has enjoyed plenty of success in it in recent years

runners.name: Green Titan
runners.spotlight: Well beaten on debut at Chepstow (6f, good to soft) 19 days ago and needs to leave that form miles behind, but he was sent off at just 100-30 and it will be worth noting whether or not his backers return.
runners.comment: Well beaten on debut but sent off just 100-30; worth noting whether his backers return

runners.name: Empire Rising
runners.spotlight: Drifted to 7-1 from 4-1 on last month's debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm), where he blew the start and was always behind; hard to fancy on the back of that but this 70,000euros yearling is likely to be a much better proposition at some point.
runners.comment: Blew the start and always behind on debut but should prove much better at some point

races.race_name: Clive Cox Racing EBF Restricted Novice Stakes (Bands B/C/D) (EBF Qual') (GBB/IRE Incentive) (Div II)

runners.name: Harry Knows
runners.spotlight: Went very close on debut at Newbury (5f, good) in April; underwhelming when odds-on and fourth of six at Goodwood (5f, good) since but he was short of room late on and has been gelded since; the step up to 6f can help and he's a strong contender if tapping back into his debut promise.
runners.comment: Went very close on debut; gelded since odds-on reverse; strong contender on initial form
runners.owner: Thames Boys, J Henwood & J Clay

runners.name: Beresford Gap
runners.spotlight: 27,000euros yearling; second foal; Acclamation half-brother to 7f AW winner Giles Glory (RPR 58); dam 7f AW 2yo winner (72), half-sister to 7f/1m winner, out of 1m winning half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Amazing Maria; yard 8-48 (17%) with 2yos here in last five years; could go well on debut.
runners.comment: Yard 8-48 (17%) with 2yos here in last five years; could go well on debut

runners.name: Aphra Behn
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; Territories half-sister to French winners Sandon (1m2f 2yo) and Betty Nelson (10.5f); dam French 1m2f winner, half-sister to French 1m2f/10.5f winner, out of maiden sister to 1m2f Group 2 winner Ana Marie; races against males on debut but trainer does very well here with his 2yos.
runners.comment: Races against males on debut but trainer does very well here with his 2yos

runners.name: Invicta Rose
runners.spotlight: 16,000euros yearling, £25,000 2yo breeze-ups; by State Of Rest; eighth foal; closely related to 7f/1m winner Tam Lin (RPR 73), half-sister to French 12.5f winner; dam 6f AW 2yo winner (69), half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 1 winner Chelsea Rose; takes on males on debut and may be one for later on.
runners.comment: £25,000 2yo breeze-ups; takes on males on debut and may be one for later on

runners.name: Goodwood Meteor
runners.spotlight: 48,000gns yearling; by Minzaal; third foal; dam unraced sister to winners Taajub (useful 5f/6f) and Excel Yourself (5f/6f 2yo), out of unraced half-sister to two Group winners; likely to improve for this debut run.
runners.comment: 48,000gns yearling; dam from talented family; likely to improve for this debut run

runners.name: Green Dividend
runners.spotlight: 30,000gns foal, 40,000gns yearling; first foal; dam placed 1m2f (RPR 71), half-sister to winners Advertise (6f/6.5f Group 1 including 2yo) and Flavius Titus (useful 6f/7f including 2yo); trainer among the winners with 2yos; the betting could be informative regarding this Showcasing newcomer.
runners.comment: Trainer among the winners with his 2yos; the betting could be informative on debut

runners.name: No More Pino
runners.spotlight: 47,000gns yearling, 40,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; second foal; dam 7f winner (including 2yo; RPR 95), half-sister to several winner, out of 5f 2yo winner; wears a hood and this Blue Point newcomer may be best watched.
runners.comment: 40,000gns 2yo breeze-ups; wears a hood on debut and he may be best watched

runners.name: Flying Pirate
runners.spotlight: 40,000euros yearling; first foal; dam US Graded stakes-placed 1m turf 2yo winner (RPR 98); in-form trainer can ready a 2yo first time up; this Blackbeard newcomer could be in the mix.
runners.comment: 40,000euros yearling; in-form trainer can ready a 2yo first time up; could be in the mix

runners.name: Blue Skies Above
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on his first two starts (6f, AW/good) and considerable improvement is needed if he's to pose a threat.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten on his first two runs (6f, AW/good) and considerable improvement is needed

runners.name: Walbrook
runners.spotlight: 58,000euros yearling; seventh foal; Cotai Glory brother to 6f-1m winner Alpha Capture (including 2yo Listed), half-brother to three winners including Acklam Express (5f including 2yo Listed; 113); trainer is enjoying a good season but he's not known for 2yo winners; check the betting on debut.
runners.comment: Attractive pedigree; trainer is enjoying a good season but he's not known for 2yo winners

runners.name: Make No Mistake
runners.spotlight: £20,000 yearling; eighth foal; closely related to US/Canadian 6.5f/7f winner Oops, half-brother to 5f/5.7f winner Suanni (including 2yo; RPR 83); dam placed in Germany, sister to Group-placed 5f 2yo winner Tawaabb; trainer has fine recent record in this race, which features his name in the title, so this Magna Grecia newcomer is well worth a second look.
runners.comment: Newcomer; trainer has fine recent record in the race, which features his name in the title

runners.name: Rattenbury
runners.spotlight: Slowly away and always behind when 28-1 for last Monday's debut at Lingfield (6f, good); he may have learnt from that experience but a hefty step forward is needed this afternoon.
runners.comment: Slowly away and always behind when 28-1 for last Monday's debut at Lingfield

races.race_name: Remembering Jane Samuels Handicap

runners.name: Phoenix Moon
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on the AW at Lingfield (6f) but she's posted good efforts on turf on her last two starts, latest when the blindfold came off late at Bath (5.5f, firm) two weeks ago; slower ground probably wouldn't be ideal.
runners.comment: All four wins over 6f at Lingfield (AW); in good form but slow ground would be a concern

runners.name: Arantes Nascimento
runners.spotlight: Unraced on soft; 2-14 on the AW (wins over 7f) compared to 0-11 on turf but it was a good effort when second of ten at Newmarket (6f, good) 24 days ago; contender if handling conditions.
runners.comment: 0-11 on turf (2-14 on AW) but it was a good effort at Newmarket last time; contender

runners.name: Unico
runners.spotlight: 3-27, the most recent of those wins coming at Brighton (5.5f, soft) in September; caught wide at Kempton on her first start for 137 days but now has the cheekpieces back on and may take a step forward.
runners.comment: Has won on soft and good to firm; caught wide at Kempton last time; cheekpieces back on

runners.name: Distant Rumble
runners.spotlight: Unraced on soft; runner-up over this C&D last month before going on better at Windsor on good to firm 17 days ago; still fairly treated following a 3lb rise but testing ground would be a concern.
runners.comment: Still fairly treated following a 3lb rise for Windsor but soft ground would be a concern

runners.name: Supreme King
runners.spotlight: Five of his six wins have been on the AW, the most recent at Kempton in February when completing a hat-trick; sole win on turf came at Doncaster (good to soft) and he posted a respectable effort there 24 days ago; drops in grade with claims.
runners.comment: Five of his six wins have been on AW but ran respectably on turf last time; down in grade

runners.name: Lodge
runners.spotlight: Won at Ffos Las (6f, good) on his debut last August before a solid effort under a penalty at Windsor (6f, soft); respectable handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in January but well beaten in two starts since; tongue-tie applied.
runners.comment: Respectable handicap debut in January but well beaten in two starts since; tongue-tied

runners.name: Invincible Speed
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been on the AW but he does act on turf; beaten 4l when second of five at Lingfield (6f, good to firm) ten days ago but soft ground would be a slight concern.
runners.comment: All four wins have been on the AW but does act on turf; soft ground a slight concern

runners.name: Addictive
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden who was last of 11 at Windsor (6f, good to firm) on his stable debut following 627 days ago; never featured over 5f at Lingfield three weeks ago but this step back up in trip may help.
runners.comment: 0-6; never featured over 5f at Lingfield but step back up in trip may help; up in grade

runners.name: Newsreader
runners.spotlight: All three wins came on the AW when trained by Kevin Philippart De Foy but he's twice been placed on turf since joining this stable, including on soft; well held at Pontefract (6f, good) in April but may have needed that first start for 194 days.
runners.comment: Three-time turf winner but twice placed on turf; may have needed his run at Pontefract

runners.name: Lazzar
runners.spotlight: All four wins have been over 6f on the AW, the most recent at Kempton in March; below his best on turf in recent starts but did have an excuse (saddle slipped) at Bath four days ago; best watched.
runners.comment: All four wins on the AW and he's been below his best on turf recently; best watched

runners.name: Thunderous Love
runners.spotlight: Six wins include two over C&D and she was runner-up here on heavy ground in September off a 5lb higher mark than today's; below her best in recent starts but probably wasn't helped by racing on the wing at Goodwood four days ago; chance.
runners.comment: Dual C&D winner, including on soft; below her best recently but had an excuse last time

races.race_name: Danny McNab Bookmakers Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Bright Summer
runners.spotlight: Built upon the promise of her debut fourth at Newbury (7f, good) in April when winning at Lingfield (7f, AW) last month; carries a penalty against some interesting rivals but did it nicely last time and she's respected.
runners.comment: Won by 2l at Lingfield; carries a penalty against some interesting rivals but respected

runners.name: Areti
runners.spotlight: 0-4 and there's a question mark regarding slow ground, but she's been runner-up on her last two starts (7f/1m, AW) and her form reads well, albeit with others probably possessing greater scope to improve; a tongue-tie goes on; could again be thereabouts if returning from a break at the top of her game.
runners.comment: 0-4 but runner-up on last two starts and could again be thereabouts

runners.name: Hugh
runners.spotlight: Encouraging fourth on debut at Kempton (6f, AW) in April and close second at Southwell (6f, AW) later that month, having led; he wasn't stopping at the line last time and 7f may well be within range; might not be far away.
runners.comment: Runner-up on second start (6f, AW) and wasn't stopping at the line; 7f may be within range

runners.name: Lucky Luna
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for fourth over 6f here (good) on last month's debut; backwards step when sixth at Chester (7f, good) since but the sharp track may not have suited; each-way player if tapping back into her debut promise.
runners.comment: Kept on for 4th here on debut; backward step since but each-way shout on initial promise

runners.name: Sapphire Secret
runners.spotlight: Unable to pose a threat this spring on her first two starts (7f/8.6f, good/AW); however, she's shown signs of promise and there can be optimism that this well-bred filly will prove to be progressive; chance.
runners.comment: Has shown signs of promise and this well-bred filly could prove to be progressive; chance

runners.name: Rajiba
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far when fourth on debut at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last November and reappeared with good second there (8.6f, AW) last Monday; drops back in trip today but it was a slowly run race last time; a possible.
runners.comment: She's run well at Wolverhampton on her two starts (6f/8.6f, AW) and she's a possible

runners.name: Bold Shout
runners.spotlight: 10-1, showed promise when fourth of ten on last Monday's debut at Newbury (6f, good to firm), beaten just under 3l; there's potential in his pedigree and he could take a step forward today.
runners.comment: Showed promise on last Monday's debut; potential in pedigree and could take a step forward

runners.name: Le Samourai
runners.spotlight: 450,000gns yearling; sixth foal; half-brother to five winners including Romantic Warrior (6f-1m2f including multiple Group/Grade 1s) and End Of Romance (6f-1m; RPR 99); the sole newcomer but he's in excellent hands and could play a leading role, especially if strong in the betting.
runners.comment: 450,000gns yearling; sole newcomer but could play a leading role; watch the betting

runners.name: Back At One
runners.spotlight: Short of room soon after the start and always towards rear when 10-1 for recent debut at Kempton (7f, AW); however, his trainer has won two of the last five runnings and this 160,000euros yearling is open to improvement.
runners.comment: Always towards rear on recent debut but trainer has won two of the last five runnings

runners.name: Siddal
runners.spotlight: Down the field at Newbury (6.5f, soft) last October on sole 2yo start and same story when 80-1 at Kempton (7f, AW) a fortnight ago; hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Down the field at Newbury on sole 2yo start and same story at Kempton a fortnight ago

runners.name: Dupont Circle
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at triple-figure odds on both starts (1m, AW).
runners.comment: Well beaten at triple-figure odds on both starts (1m, AW)
runners.owner: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club

runners.name: Jean's Boy
runners.spotlight: It's early days for this half-brother to three winners but he's been beaten a long way at 40-1 and 100-1 on his first two starts.
runners.comment: Early days but beaten a long way at 40-1 and 100-1 on his first two starts

runners.name: Paint Your Palette
runners.spotlight: Tailed off for this yard in two conditions races on dirt at Meydan (7f/1m) in the winter; went through the sales ring for 2,000gns in April; dramatic improvement is needed on her first turf start.
runners.comment: Twice tailed off on the Meydan dirt in the winter; dramatic improvement is needed

runners.name: Cosmic Jive
runners.spotlight: He has a useful pedigree and may have needed his debut experience at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in March, when 11-1, but he was beaten a long way and a transformation is necessary this afternoon.
runners.comment: May have needed debut experience in March but a transformation is necessary this afternoon

races.race_name: Kingsclere Racing Club Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Besieged
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty when a 1l second at Kempton (1m, AW) on his debut in April and built on that when second of 11 at Newbury (1m2f, good) 25 days ago; dam finished runner-up in a Group 1 on testing ground; sets the standard.
runners.comment: Runner-up in both starts (1m/1m2f); dam was second in a Group 1 on testing ground

runners.name: Poor Relation
runners.spotlight: Caught the eye when fifth of 11 on her debut at Lingfield (1m, AW) in December and built on that when runner-up at Southwell (1m, AW) 140 days ago, despite hanging left; steps up in trip on her first start since and she's open to further progress.
runners.comment: Built on her eye-catching debut when runner-up at Southwell 140 days ago; steps up in trip

runners.name: Last Trump
runners.spotlight: Shaped with promise at Newbury (1m3f, good) in April but wasn't able to build on that when fourth of eight here over 1m4f (good to firm) 37 days ago; drops to 1m2f and looks the type to do better when handicapping.
runners.comment: Shaped with promise on debut (1m3f) but didn't build on that here over 1m4f last time

runners.name: Centrum
runners.spotlight: Colt by Dubawi; thirteenth foal; half-brother to eight winners including Enable (1m-1m4f including 2yo/AW/Oaks/dual Arc; RPR 129), Derab (Listed-placed 1m; 115) and Contribution (1m4f/1m7f; 106); dam 1m2f winner (including Listed; 102), sister to 12.5f Group 2 winner Dance Routine; bred to be smart and he's of interest on debut.
runners.comment: Dubawi colt; half-brother to eight winners including dual Arc winner Enable; lots to like

runners.name: Herstmonceux
runners.spotlight: Gelding by Ulysses; tenth foal; half-brother to winners Timothy T (7.6f 2yo; RPR 92), Poetic Light (7.7f 2yo; 83), Suitsus (6f AW/1m turf; 74) and Equal Point (6f AW; 66); dam unplaced 6.5f/7f 2yo (35), out of sister to 1m2f/2m Listed winner Upper Strata; wears blinkers for his debut.
runners.comment: Gelding by Ulysses; half-brother to four winners; wears blinkers for his debut

runners.name: Much
runners.spotlight: Well held in eighth in a race won by Derby runner-up Maltese Cross at Newbury (1m2f, good) on his debut and never featured over that same C&D 25 days ago; pedigree suggests slightly further may suit, and handicaps may be more his thing.
runners.comment: Well held in eighth on his debut and never featured last time; handicaps may be his thing

runners.name: Traveling Man
runners.spotlight: Gelding by Too Darn Hot; 360,000gns yearling; third foal; closely related to winners Al Musmak (7f-1m1f including 2yo/dirt/Listed; RPR 113) and Mahshoom (KSA 1m1f); dam unraced half-sister to useful 1m1f-1m4f winner Boltaway, out of quadruple US 1m/1m1f Grade 1 winner; nice pedigree and the market should be informative on his debut.
runners.comment: Gelded; 360,000gns yearling; closely related to Listed winner Al Musmak; watch the betting
runners.owner: P Hickman And Lord Lloyd Webber

runners.name: Easwrith Destiny
runners.spotlight: 200-1 when beaten 22l on his debut at Newbury (1m2f, good) in April and again finished well beaten after racing freely at Chepstow (1m2f, good to soft) 19 days ago; probably one for handicaps later on.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two starts at Newbury and Chepstow; probably one for handicaps later on

runners.name: Notable Charm
runners.spotlight: Struggled on his debut at Newbury (1m, good; 150-1) in April and it was the same story at Lingfield (1m, AW) 28 days ago; pedigree suggests this step up in trip will suit but handicaps may be more suitable.
runners.comment: Struggled in two starts over 1m (turf/AW); steps up in trip but handicaps more suitable

runners.name: Long Reign
runners.spotlight: Showed little on his debut at Newbury (1m, good; 125-1; subsequently gelded) and again finished well beaten at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) 29 days ago; not easy to recommend.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two starts (gelded before latest) and he isn't easy to recommend

runners.name: Cartrefle
runners.spotlight: Tailed off when last of seven on his debut at Redcar (1m1f, good to soft) in October and he again failed to beat a rival home at Southwell (1m, AW) next time; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Failed to beat a rival home in both starts last year (1m1f/1m); others are preferred

races.race_name: Everywhen Margadale Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Rossa Raheen
runners.spotlight: Ran to a similar level in two starts last year for David Menuisier (1m, soft/good to soft) and posted an improved effort despite not getting the best of runs in a handicap at Newbury (1m2f, good) 25 days ago on her second start for this stable; unexposed and looks a big player.
runners.comment: Posted an improved effort on her handicap debut at Newbury (1m2f); big player

runners.name: Jazzy Baby
runners.spotlight: Sole win from eight starts came in a maiden at Kempton (1m, AW) in January and she's posted some solid efforts there in handicaps since; however, she now steps up in trip following a poor effort there 40 days ago and needs to bounce back on her turf debut.
runners.comment: Sole win came in a maiden at Kempton (1m) but disappointing latest; steps up in trip

runners.name: Royal Poetry
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the third attempt when making all in a novice at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in April before finishing a good second of four over this C&D (good to firm) on her handicap debut; however, she was disappointing at Newmarket 24 days ago and needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Good second of four over C&D on her handicap debut but disappointing last time
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

runners.name: Morven
runners.spotlight: Won a novice at Hamilton (1m, good to soft) in September on her second start but was well beaten in a Listed event at Pontefract (1m, soft) next time; good effort when beaten a head at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) on her reappearance and has solid claims if seeing out this longer trip.
runners.comment: Disappointed in a Listed event on her final start at two but ran well on her return (8.5f)

runners.name: Affection
runners.spotlight: Previously trained in France and she's won four of her ten starts, the most recent win coming by a neck in a claimer at Salon-de-Provence (1m4f, good to soft) 54 days ago; drops back in trip for her stable/British debut and the betting should be informative.
runners.comment: Won four of her ten starts in France, the most recent coming over 1m4f; stable debut

runners.name: Albus Anne
runners.spotlight: All five of her wins have been at Bath (1m/1m2f) with four of them on soft/heavy, the latest over 1m2f in October off a 4lb lower mark than today's; third in both starts over this C&D and she's proven fresh, so is of some interest on her first start for 188 days.
runners.comment: All five wins have been at Bath but third in both starts over this C&D; goes well on soft

runners.name: Regal Charm
runners.spotlight: Won a novice at Newmarket (1m, good to firm) last August on her second start and ran well in a handicap at Newbury (1m2f, soft) 228 days ago; off since but she should have a bit more to offer after just five starts.
runners.comment: Won a 1m novice at Newmarket last year; ran well over 1m2f on her h'cap debut 228 days ago

runners.name: Typical Woman
runners.spotlight: Unraced on soft but handles good to soft; below her best in three starts this year but now drops back in grade off the same mark as when winning at Lingfield (1m1f, good to firm) last year; returning cheekpieces are another plus.
runners.comment: Below her best this year but drops in grade off her last winning mark; cheekpieces back on

races.race_name: Charter 1227 Catering Handicap

runners.name: Marra Donna
runners.spotlight: Made it 0-9 and he wasn't at his best (hung badly left) on a rare turf start at Windsor (6f, good; fifth) three weeks ago; needs to prove he can get the job done but he's on a handy mark on his consistent AW form earlier this year; not written off.
runners.comment: 0-9 and below par on turf last time but on a handy mark on this year's consistent AW form

runners.name: Maid In Devon
runners.spotlight: Built upon first two handicap performances to win in a big field over C&D last month (good; Stock Market second); 5lb higher today and slower ground is forecast, but she may have more to offer now things have clicked.
runners.comment: Won in big field over C&D last month; may have more to offer now things have clicked

runners.name: Madman
runners.spotlight: Won at Kempton (6f, AW) in January and he's on a good mark on his peak AW form; however, he hasn't been at his best back on turf on his first two outings for this stable, and is 0-9 on grass.
runners.comment: Won on AW in January but below best on turf on first two outings for this stable

runners.name: Tenzi
runners.spotlight: Showed promise on turf last summer (good/soft ) and won a novice at Southwell (7f, AW) this March; however, she hasn't posed a serious threat in two handicaps subsequently (6f, AW/good to firm) and needs something extra today.
runners.comment: Won AW novice in March but hasn't posed a serious threat in handicaps subsequently

runners.name: Grand Vista
runners.spotlight: He's shown hints of promise among his three starts (including on soft ground) and his pedigree provides optimism that he can prove better than his opening mark; wears first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: Pedigree provides hope that he can prove better than his opening mark; blinkers go on

runners.name: Grimese's Special
runners.spotlight: She's been very disappointing on her three starts (1m/7f, AW/good) since winning a seller here (7f, heavy) last September on her second start; the return to slow ground needs to prompt a dramatic revival.
runners.comment: Heavy-ground seller win here last September; return to slow ground needs to prompt revival

runners.name: Barry The Worm
runners.spotlight: Off the mark at the seventh attempt when wearing first-time cheekpieces (retained since) in a nursery at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) last November; April's reappearance sixth at Southwell (6f, AW) gives him something to build on and he's a possible contender if the return to turf isn't an issue.
runners.comment: AW nursery win on final 2yo starts and reappearance sixth gives him something to build on

runners.name: Stock Market
runners.spotlight: 12-race maiden who failed to settle when below par at Chepstow (5f, good to soft) latest; however, seven days earlier he was a close second to Maid In Devon over C&D (good) and can be thereabouts if bouncing back.
runners.comment: 0-12 and below his best last time, but close second of 15 over C&D two starts ago; chance

runners.name: Yes Waliim
runners.spotlight: 0-5 and unable to make an impact this year in his first two handicaps; however, he made late headway when 4l sixth of eight at Lingfield last time (5f, good; unraced on softer) and might have a part to play back up to 6f.
runners.comment: Late headway when 6th of eight over 5f last time; might have a part to play back up to 6f

runners.name: Wayward Queen
runners.spotlight: She's achieved little this spring on her first three starts (6f, good to firm/AW/good); however, she's with a top trainer and goes handicapping off a basement mark in first-time headgear; interesting contender.
runners.comment: She's achieved little but is with a top trainer and goes handicapping off a basement mark

runners.name: Follow My Heart
runners.spotlight: No match for stablemate Maid In Devon and Stock Market when fourth of 15 on last month's handicap debut over C&D (good), which came after a break; she has a useful pedigree and is open to improvement, especially if her efforts are channeled in a straight line (hung right last time).
runners.comment: Fourth of 15 over C&D on last month's handicap debut and she's open to improvement

runners.name: That's Random
runners.spotlight: He's not obviously well treated off this opening mark but has shown some ability among his first three starts (7f/6f, AW) and is bred to be better than this grade; no surprise if he takes a sizeable step forward.
runners.comment: Has shown some ability and is bred to be better than his opening mark

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Huckleberry Sting
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 7yo who tends to race too freely but displayed considerable improvement when upped to 3m and switched to fences at Bangor (good) in April, winning by 5l; beaten about 21l when second over C&D last month but gave the all-the-way winner far too much rope that day, and his margin of defeat was magnified by late jumping errors; remains of strong interest.
runners.comment: Won when upped to 3m on chase debut in April and not discredited here last time

runners.name: Another Mystery
runners.spotlight: Returned to racing under rules with win in four-runner chase at Fakenham (3m, good) in April, in which his three rivals toiled and were pulled up; it's hard to gauge what that form is worth and his overall profile is not compelling but he clocked a good time (despite being eased right down).
runners.comment: Finished alone at Fakenham in April; the form is hard to weigh up but the time was good

runners.name: Milan Milos
runners.spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but battled on well to make winning chase debut in four-runner contest at Plumpton (3m1f, good; first-time tongue-tie) in April and was nudged up only 2lb for that; remains unexposed beyond 2m4f; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Battled on well to make winning chase debut at Plumpton; still unexposed as a stayer
runners.owner: Team Payne, Powell, Ray And Warburton

runners.name: Anytrixwilldo
runners.spotlight: 1-15 over fences; runner-up twice in cheekpieces at Ludlow (3m, good to soft) at start of this year but didn't fire in first-time blinkers (retained) at Fontwell last month; others are more persuasive.
runners.comment: Runner-up twice at start of this year but has modest strike-rate and didn't fire last time

runners.name: Limerick Leader
runners.spotlight: In good form for Alison Thorpe in early part of last season, notably when winning at Newton Abbot (2m5f, good); yet fully to convince stamina-wise at around 3m and has fitness to prove on this stable debut (same owners) but Sean Bowen (who has won on him before) is booked; swaps visor for first-time blinkers.
runners.comment: In good form in early part of last season; Sean Bowen booked for stable debut; interesting

runners.name: Pleasure Garden
runners.spotlight: Ran well for a long way before fading into fourth at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) last month but is effectively 7lb higher from out of the weights here and it's over three years since his only chase win; tries a new trip.
runners.comment: Gained only chase win in 2023 and is 9lb wrong here; tries a new trip

runners.name: No More No
runners.spotlight: Won over hurdles here in October 2024 and returned from seven-month break with wide-margin C&D (good) success on chasing debut last May; absent for another 13 months since and now 13lb higher but remains unexposed over fences and can evidently go well when fresh.
runners.comment: Absent since 31l win over C&D on chase debut last May, but that also came after a layoff

runners.name: Friends Don't Ask
runners.spotlight: Four-time chase winner; no longer the force of old but returned from break with three placed runs last month, losing out only by a short head at Fontwell (3m2f, good) last time; can make his presence felt again.
runners.comment: Back from break with three placed runs last month; can make presence felt again

runners.name: Wings On Wheels
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed under rules and was seemingly aided by new cheekpieces when second of four at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) in March; that form isn't anything to get excited about, though, and he was well held when third at Perth (2m4f, good to soft) last month; needs improvement over this new trip.
runners.comment: Still unexposed under rules but needs improvement over this new trip

races.race_name: Sun Racing Summer Jumps Championship Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Beorma
runners.spotlight: Fast-improving mare who won easily on chasing debut at Worcester (2m, good; first-time tongue-tie) 12 days ago and has quickly added two easy hurdle wins (2m4f/2m, good) to her tally; probably still a long way ahead of the handicapper and can continue her winning spree here.
runners.comment: Fast-improving mare who has collected three easy wins in the last 12 days

runners.name: Catchim
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who was left clear just after final fence to score at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) in April, opening his chase account at the 12th attempt, but needs to bounce back quickly from a poor run there 12 days ago.
runners.comment: Left clear to open chase account at Market Rasen in April; well beaten there since

runners.name: Alshadhian
runners.spotlight: Won this race by 13l a year ago and was still in good form when last seen last summer/autumn; has fitness to prove after eight-month absence, though, and current mark probably demands a career-best performance.
runners.comment: Won this in 2025 but has fitness to prove after 236-day break and is on fairly tough mark
runners.owner: G C Myddelton & V E C Taylor

runners.name: Kill The Groove
runners.spotlight: Now 9lb lower than when going close over hurdles at Plumpton (2m, soft) in January but was safely held in both chases this spring (2m3f/2m), and cheekpieces didn't appear to help much when used once previously.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on hurdle second in January but safely held in both chases this spring

runners.name: Electric Eddy
runners.spotlight: Kept on well for second on chase debut at Hereford (2m, good) in November but was pulled up on next outing and made low-key reappearance when third of five (probably only fourth best on merit) at Worcester (2m, good) last month; needs to up his game here.
runners.comment: Good second on chase debut in November but below that form twice since; others preferred

runners.name: Walk The Plank
runners.spotlight: Battled well for narrow success at Huntingdon (2m, good) in March, his third chase start, and gave another good account before fading into fourth at Market Rasen (2m1f, good) 12 days ago; might collect a minor prize.
runners.comment: Battled well to win at Huntingdon in March and also ran well 12 days ago; place claims

runners.name: Fond Farewell
runners.spotlight: Won a low-grade Flat handicap in Ireland in 2023 but unplaced all six hurdle runs for current stable; not beaten far when fifth of six at Worcester (2m, good) recently but not bred for chasing; others are much safer.
runners.comment: Not beaten far over hurdles recently but not bred for chasing; others are much safer

races.race_name: Southwell Novices' Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Cher Monsieur
runners.spotlight: Scored over hurdles at start of this year and built upon his promising chase debut when winning four-runner race at Plumpton (2m4f, good) a fortnight ago; only 3lb higher here and probably still on the upgrade; wears tongue-tie for first time; good chance.
runners.comment: Raised only 3lb for recent Plumpton win and probably still on the upgrade; good chance

runners.name: Manowest
runners.spotlight: Just 1-12 over hurdles and ran poorly when upped to 3m2f at Perth last month; makes chasing debut from a workable mark, though, and his Irish point win in 2024 bodes well for his prospects over fences.
runners.comment: Just 1-12 over hurdles and disappointed last time but makes chase debut from workable mark

runners.name: Thank You Blue
runners.spotlight: Returned to form and completed quick hat-trick over hurdles in February (2m-2m4f, good to soft/soft) but quickly went off the boil towards end of last season (pulled up on two of last three appearances); competes off his lower chase mark here and might be allowed an uncontested lead but has a poor completion rate over fences; risky.
runners.comment: Won three hurdles in February but quickly went off boil afterwards; risky on chase return

runners.name: Nazare
runners.spotlight: Lost his way after winning twice over hurdles in November (2m4f, good to soft; once here) and was a disappointing favourite when well-held third of four on last month's chasing debut at Ludlow (3m, good); may yet turn things around but comes here with quite a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Has lost way since his two hurdle wins in November; well held when third on chase debut

races.race_name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Maiden Hurdle (ARC Summer Novices' Brush Hurdle Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Manalishi
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on last year's bumper debut and placed on all three hurdling starts, most recently when clear second of eight at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good) last month; leading contender.
runners.comment: Placed all three hurdling starts, most recently when clear second at Uttoxeter last month

runners.name: Hawk's Rock
runners.spotlight: Irish raider who launched hurdling career with two promising runs in cheekpieces last month, latterly when close fourth of ten at Cartmel (2m6f, good); that form already puts him on the premises here, and he can improve if he cuts out the jumping errors.
runners.comment: Launched hurdling career with two promising runs last month; in the mix again here

runners.name: Who's Glen
runners.spotlight: Useful Flat racer who made the frame on four of his five hurdling starts for Donald McCain last season, most notably when third in valuable series final at Musselburgh (2m4f, good to soft) in March; well beaten in good-quality Flat handicap last month but ought to play a significant role in this.
runners.comment: Placed in valuable series final in March and ought to play a significant role in this.

runners.name: Granite Sands
runners.spotlight: Bettered low-key hurdle debut when keeping on for modest third of six at Stratford (2m6f, good to firm) last month but needs to take another good step forward here.
runners.comment: Second hurdle run was better than the debut but another big step forward is needed

runners.name: Rock Sensation
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground course bumper for Ben Pauling in spring 2025 and made the frame in soft-ground Listed bumper at Cheltenham in November but has made an underwhelming start to her hurdling career; shaped with some promise on last month's stable debut at Fontwell (2m1f, good) but was safely held in the end; needs to up her game over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Looked quite useful in bumpers but has made underwhelming start to hurdling career

runners.name: Run Happy
runners.spotlight: Made quite promising rules debut when fifth of 14 in pointers' bumper at Aintree (good) last month but almost certainly needs to improve to threaten the principals on this hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Fifth of 14 in pointers' bumper on rules debut at Aintree; improvement needed here
runners.owner: The Hon A Vestey, Sangwin & J Scutt

runners.name: Jacquette
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at huge odds on both hurdling starts for Danni O'Neill; now with Georgie Howell.
runners.comment: Well beaten at huge odds on both hurdling starts for Danni O'Neill; now with new stable

runners.name: Papillondelumiere
runners.spotlight: Maiden pointer who was soundly beaten when 100-1 for first two hurdling starts.
runners.comment: Maiden pointer who was soundly beaten when 100-1 for first two hurdling starts

runners.name: Trigger The Light
runners.spotlight: Made quite promising rules debut when third in seven-runner Warwick bumper (good) in October but was pulled up on hurdling debut later that month (2m4f, good to soft); had wind op during seven-month break.
runners.comment: Third in a bumper in October but pulled up on hurdle debut; had wind op during layoff

races.race_name: Vaughandale Construction 'Built To Last' Novices' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Collanisi
runners.spotlight: Made solid start to handicap career when close third of eight at Uttoxeter (2m, good) last month and any improvement on that performance will give him a good shout here.
runners.comment: Made solid start to handicap hurdle career when third at Uttoxeter last month; respected

runners.name: Paragon
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging handicap debut over C&D (good) last month and his recent fourth in first-time cheekpieces at Market Rasen (2m, good) was also respectable; needs to find something extra today, though.
runners.comment: First two handicap runs were respectable but he needs to find something extra today

runners.name: Red Trail
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced when fifth of seven (behind Paragon) at Market Rasen (2m, good) 12 days ago, after a break, but is now 0-15 over hurdles and her overall profile is not appealing.
runners.comment: Not disgraced at Market Rasen recently but now 0-15 over hurdles; others are much stronger

runners.name: Phantom Gold
runners.spotlight: Did not get competitive in three maiden/novice hurdles (2m-2m4f) for new stable this spring, following a long absence, but won a bumper by 9l in summer 2023 and there's still a chance some of that ability will resurface at some point; watch the betting.
runners.comment: Did not get competitive in three maiden/novice hurdles; may do better in handicaps, though

runners.name: Prettylady
runners.spotlight: Made low-key start to British hurdling career last season but delivered on promise shown on the Flat in France when winning from off the pace on last month's handicap hurdle debut at Stratford (2m, good to firm); could easily have more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Displayed improvement to win on last month's handicap hurdle debut; can progress again

runners.name: Versatile
runners.spotlight: Displayed promise when making the frame in two maiden hurdles (2m, good/good to firm) this spring but he refused to race on the Flat in between and needs to improve to overcome top weight on this handicap hurdle debut.
runners.comment: Showed promise when making the frame on first two hurdling starts; needs improvement here

runners.name: Littletown Lad
runners.spotlight: Unable to justify prominence in the betting when soundly beaten in first two handicaps, the latter over C&D last month, and his tendency to race too freely remains a worry; evidently thought capable of better, though.
runners.comment: Unable to justify prominence in the betting when soundly beaten in first two handicaps

runners.name: Caspada
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on first two hurdling starts but belied 150-1 odds when second in 14-runner Chepstow maiden (2m, good) in April and has plenty of scope for further improvement in handicaps.
runners.comment: Belied big odds when second in 14-runner Chepstow maiden in April; handicap debut today

runners.name: Global Hideaway
runners.spotlight: Bled from the nose on final two starts for Dan Skelton, latterly when pulled-up 66-1 shot on handicap debut just under a year ago; now with Christian Williams (same owner); others have much less to prove.
runners.comment: Pulled up when making handicap debut for Dan Skelton; makes stable debut after layoff

runners.name: Brooklyn Lullaby
runners.spotlight: Showed no significant promise when a big-priced outsider for three maiden hurdles last August/September and makes handicap debut after a 255-day absence; 2lb wrong; of interest only if attracting market support.
runners.comment: Showed no significant promise when a big-priced outsider for three maiden hurdles in 2025

runners.name: Beaufort Breeze
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner in spring 2024; well beaten at triple-digit odds in three novice/maiden hurdles in 2024-25 season (2m-2m4f) but two of those were good-quality races at Cheltenham; makes handicap debut after a long absence but remains unexposed and is worth a glance in the betting.
runners.comment: Absent since three low-key qualifying runs in 2024-25 season; still worth a market glance

races.race_name: Free Racenight Thursday 18th June Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Callin Baton Rouge
runners.spotlight: Not quite at her best this year but has dropped another 2lb since last month's respectable third at Huntingdon (2m, good to firm) and this move back up in trip won't be a problem; probably won't be far away.
runners.comment: Not quite at best this year but has dropped another 2lb since last month's third

runners.name: Gone In Sixty
runners.spotlight: Returned from break with low-key chase run last month and fell mid-race when back over hurdles nine days ago but remains on a good mark and will be suited by today's trip/ground; can't be ruled out.
runners.comment: Fell at Fakenham recently but remains on a good mark and conditions here will suit

runners.name: Ruler Legend
runners.spotlight: Ended last season in excellent form, winning twice at Market Rasen (2m/2m2f, good), and his second at Market Rasen (2m, good) 12 days ago was another good effort; should be fine over this longer trip; respected.
runners.comment: Won twice at Market Rasen in April and his recent second there was another good effort

runners.name: Royal Rhythm
runners.spotlight: Without a win since December 2023 but lost out only by a nose at Worcester (2m4f, good) last summer and returned from break with satisfactory run at Fontwell last month; contender if that recent outing brought him on.
runners.comment: Returned from break with satisfactory run last month; contender if that brought him on

runners.name: Achille Des Rocs
runners.spotlight: Dual hurdle winner (2m/2m3f, good) towards end of 2024; not yet fully exposed but returned from one-year absence with disappointing chase run in April and needs to get career back on track.
runners.comment: Dual hurdle winner in 2024; returned from layoff with disappointing chase run in April

runners.name: Fighting Falcon
runners.spotlight: Low-mileage 5yo who made winning stable debut when back under rules at Uttoxeter (2m4f, good; first-time cheekpieces) last month; drew well clear of the others with the runner-up there and could still be well treated after a 6lb rise.
runners.comment: Made winning stable debut when back under rules last month and remains lightly raced

runners.name: Golden Glance
runners.spotlight: Out of form since returning from long absence in December, albeit her seventh of eight at Aintree (2m4f, good; first run after wind op) last month was arguably more encouraging than bare form suggests; others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Out of form since returning from long absence in December; needs to turn a corner

runners.name: William Of York
runners.spotlight: Regressive 10yo who has poor completion rate over fences since returning from layoff in November; stopped the rot with fairly respectable hurdle run last month but needs to build on that performance here; swaps visor for refitted cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Regressive 10yo; latest run stopped the rot but he needs to build on that effort here

races.race_name: Celebrate With Sponsorship At Southwell Racecourse Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Copper Beach
runners.spotlight: Came from off the pace to take second twice last month (2m4f/2m3f, good; tongue-tie added), after a long layoff, and is likely to be suited by this move back up in trip; must be considered.
runners.comment: Second twice last month (2m4f/2m3f), after a layoff, and this longer trip should suit

runners.name: Ikigai Star
runners.spotlight: Ran okay when third of five at Fontwell (2m1f, good to soft) in April but was pulled up on latest outing, and his Flat pedigree raises doubts about the suitability of this new trip; swaps cheekpieces for refitted visor.
runners.comment: Ran okay two starts ago but pulled up since and has stamina to prove today

runners.name: Fairly Fulling
runners.spotlight: Came good over hurdles at 14th attempt, with clear success at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft) in March, but ran poorly when dropped back to 2m last month and it's hard to know what to expect from him on first attempt at 3m under rules.
runners.comment: 2m4f winner in March; pulled up over 2m since; hard to predict over this new trip

runners.name: East Eagle
runners.spotlight: Showed career-best form when scoring by 9l at Stratford (3m2f, good) in April and also has a C&D success on his CV but was unable to land a telling blow on his two starts at Hexham last month and needs to kick on again to overcome current mark.
runners.comment: Good winner at Stratford in April but seemingly a shade too high in the weights now
runners.owner: Mrs Janet Cooper & M E Sowersby

runners.name: Indian Sunbird
runners.spotlight: Won twice last year on good ground (once over C&D) and is entitled to have benefited from last month's reappearance run but may find others in this field better handicapped.
runners.comment: Won twice (once over C&D) last year but may find others in this field better handicapped

runners.name: Danger Nap
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 5yo who was clear second when upped to 3m1f and fitted with first-time cheekpieces for last month's handicap debut at Warwick (good); could still have more to offer; high on the list.
runners.comment: Clear second when upped to 3m1f for last month's handicap debut; has more to offer

runners.name: Artiste d'Ainay
runners.spotlight: Easily made all in a point in April but was just a respectable fifth over hurdles at Hexham (2m7f, good) for new connections last month and needs to build on that performance here.
runners.comment: Point winner this spring; just a respectable fifth when back over hurdles last month

runners.name: Porridge
runners.spotlight: 0-14 over hurdles; excelled herself when fourth at huge odds in 2m3f Fontwell maiden in April but, having been hiked up the weights, was soundly beaten in both subsequent handicaps (2m5f/2m7f).
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in both handicaps since huge-odds maiden fourth in April

runners.name: Iamcelestialgift
runners.spotlight: Won on hurdling debut in April 2025 (2m3f) but struggled in both handicaps last season (3m/2m5f); returns from five-month break with a lot to prove.
runners.comment: Struggled in first two handicaps; returns from five-month break here
runners.owner: DL Adams, JA Adams & G McPherson

runners.name: Getaway Glory
runners.spotlight: Out of form (mainly over fences) since returning from long absence in January (had wind op before latest run); swaps cheekpieces for first-time visor and has tongue-tie refitted; revival needed.
runners.comment: Out of form since returning from long absence; headgear switched; revival needed

runners.name: Can U Feel It
runners.spotlight: 0-6 over hurdles but put in good front-running shift when third at Fakenham (2m4f, good; first-time tongue-tie) last month and is open to more improvement over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Placed over 2m4f at Fakenham last month and open to more improvement over this trip

runners.name: Stripe Of Honour
runners.spotlight: Veteran who was in good form in the early part of last season and gained a narrow C&D (good) win on latest outing in October; only 3lb higher today but has fitness to prove and younger rivals appeal more.
runners.comment: Narrow C&D winner on latest outing in October but younger rivals appeal more all the same

runners.name: L'Air du Large
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only five hurdle runs but struggled in first two handicaps (2m/2m3f; cheekpieces added last time); upped in trip again for first run since December; considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Struggled in first two handicaps (2m/2m3f); upped in trip again after a break

races.race_name: Yeats Irish Whiskey Claiming Hurdle

runners.name: Hasten Slowly
runners.spotlight: Two of her three wins over hurdles have come at Tramore; a long way below her best in a similar race to this at that course in April; hard to fancy following remote seventh behind Rich Belief at Wexford.
runners.comment: Seven career wins, hard to fancy following remote seventh behind Rich Belief at Wexford

runners.name: Torquay Road
runners.spotlight: Three-time hurdles winner, including on both Sligo visits; has been chasing and went close at Tramore on his penultimate start; strong contender here on the form that brought handicap wins over C&D and at Galway in the first half of last season; rider's 7lb claim an asset.
runners.comment: Three-time hurdles winner, has been chasing, went close on penultimate start, contender

runners.name: Raydamann
runners.spotlight: Decent form near the end of the season before last, winning a maiden hurdle at Clonmel and placed in a handicap at the Punchestown festival; fair form in handicaps last summer; subsequent form raises doubts.
runners.comment: Decent form near the end of the season before last, has plenty to prove at this stage

runners.name: Rich Belief
runners.spotlight: Fair hurdles form in 2022; has done most of his racing on the Flat in recent years; second at Clonmel last September has been the only bright spot in that sphere since a 2024 Chester win; found his level when winning in this category at Wexford 13 days ago; should be the mix again.
runners.comment: Two ordinary Flat runs before winning in this category at Wexford, should be the mix again

runners.name: Woodstream Lad
runners.spotlight: Has been inconsistent in recent years; three-time winner; returned to form with a second placing in a similar race at Tramore last month; should go close.
runners.comment: Returned to form with a second placing in a similar race at Tramore, should go close

runners.name: Phoenix Arizona
runners.spotlight: 0-7 in points; placed in two small-field maiden hurdles last year; overall form is unconvincing.
runners.comment: Placed twice in small-field maiden hurdles and in points, overall form is unconvincing

runners.name: Eyeeye
runners.spotlight: Low-rated Flat maiden with minor placed form over hurdles; lacks a recent run and is not well suited by the race conditions; others much preferred.
runners.comment: Lacks a recent run and is not well suited by the race conditions, others much preferred

runners.name: Jaariam
runners.spotlight: Fit from a point-to-point campaign; some ability in bumpers in 2022; weak hurdles form.
runners.comment: Fit from a point-to-point campaign, some ability in bumpers in 2022, weak hurdles form

runners.name: Tomas O Maille
runners.spotlight: Only one win from 26 starts over hurdles; a long way below his best in two starts in May; not well treated by the race conditions.
runners.comment: Only one win from 26 starts over hurdles, a long way below his best in two starts in May

runners.name: Bocelli's Voice
runners.spotlight: Weak chase form over the past 12 months; has a modest hurdles rating; veteran faces a stiff task in this company.
runners.comment: Weak chase form over the past 12 months, has a modest hurdles rating, stiff task here

races.race_name: Stephen Mullins & Sons Mares Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Scathach
runners.spotlight: Finished in midfield in a strong Leopardstown maiden on debut; remote sixth behind Bambino Fever at Fairyhouse in January, with today's rival Family Way behind; made mistakes and fell two out at Naas in February; tailed off when well-fancied at Wexford last month; too keen then, fair chance now if settling better.
runners.comment: Tailed off when well-fancied at Wexford, too keen then, fair chance now if settling better

runners.name: Bangor Lady
runners.spotlight: Down the field off a lowly mark in handicap hurdle runs at this venue and Tramore; very hard to make a case for her, though the stable had a 16-1 winner at Punchestown on Saturday.
runners.comment: Down the field off a lowly mark in her two handicap hurdle runs, hard to make a case

runners.name: Clash Gold
runners.spotlight: Mildly promising bumper debut in a 2024 contest won by Bambino Fever, well held in two 2m4f Fairyhouse maiden hurdles; took a small step in the right direction when fifth at Tramore in April; place chance.
runners.comment: Took a small step in the right direction when fifth at Tramore in April, place chance

runners.name: Ellens Pride
runners.spotlight: Point winner on yielding ground for Tim Doyle last November; well beaten in four starts under rules; jumped badly in a maiden hurdle at Ballinrobe a fortnight ago on first start for Sam Curling.
runners.comment: Point winner last November, jumped badly in a maiden hurdle at Ballinrobe a fortnight ago

runners.name: Carrigeen Kirkia
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in points; has failed to make any impact in three bumper starts; stout pedigree, dam a point winner and sister to Irish Grand National winner Rogue Angel.
runners.comment: Weak form in points, has failed to make any impact in three bumper starts, stout pedigree

runners.name: Cherry Red
runners.spotlight: Maiden pointer has failed to complete twice in points since return from a long absence; missed an engagement at Puchestown on Sunday.
runners.comment: Maiden pointer has failed to complete twice in points since return from a long absence

runners.name: Crios Cu Chulainn
runners.spotlight: Idaho mare out of a half-sister to Champion Hurdle winner Faugheen; tailed off on debut in a bumper at Punchestown; moderate seventh at Wexford 13 days ago; no hurdling experience.
runners.comment: Dam is a half-sister to the brilliant Faugheen, soundly beaten in two bumper runs

runners.name: Family Getaway
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to useful and versatile performer The Very Man; in touch when falling at the third-last on debut in a 2m Cork maiden hurdle last November; out of her depth in maidens won by Oldschool Outlaw and Bambino Fever.
runners.comment: Not competitive in two completed starts last winter, might be good enough in this grade

runners.name: Tawny Lea
runners.spotlight: Down the field in maidens at this venue and Limerick last month; not a likely contender.
runners.comment: Down the field in maidens at this venue and Limerick last month, not a likely contender

runners.name: Mia In Milan
runners.spotlight: Ran respectably in mid-field in Punchestown festival bumper; never in contention on hurdles debut at Killarney.
runners.comment: Ran respectably in a Punchestown festival bumper, never in contention on hurdles debut

runners.name: Mo Chailin Mor
runners.spotlight: Beaten in excess of 70l in three maiden hurdles last winter; chance is not obvious; however, she is in good hands and a tongue-tie may help.
runners.comment: Beaten in excess of 70l in three maiden hurdles last winter, chance is not obvious

races.race_name: RSS Record Storage Service Maiden Hurdle

runners.name: Killowen House
runners.spotlight: Half-brother to the talented Chantry House; off the mark at fifth attempt in points at Quakerstown in April; shaped with promise when third behind a stablemate of Koliath De Couely on hurdling debut at Cork; however, that was over a much longer trip; this might not be ideal, fair chance all the same.
runners.comment: Point winner, well-related, shaped with promise on hurdles debut at Cork, big drop in trip

runners.name: Lilac Lady
runners.spotlight: Locally-trained mare; boasts fair placed form at this venue, including second behind a well-fancied rival four weeks ago; has the potential to win a race but likely to find a few too good this time.
runners.comment: Locally-trained mare, fair placed form at this venue, likely to find a few too good

runners.name: Crystabel
runners.spotlight: Has shown improved form to take third in maidens here and at Ballinrobe; perhaps a little more is needed to make the frame here.
runners.comment: Has shown improved form to take third in maidens here and at Ballinrobe, more needed
runners.owner: M R D Fleming & G Bruce & J P Ryan & Miss G Ryan

runners.name: Hopeful Hero
runners.spotlight: Placed in four of seven bumper starts and in both starts over hurdles; seems to run best on good ground, so any ease in the ground might be a negative; may need this after a break.
runners.comment: Placed in four bumpers and in both starts over hurdles, might need this after a break

runners.name: The Boysofairhill
runners.spotlight: Point winner in a confined maiden at Dromahane; tailed off in a bumper at Listowel last week; no hurdling experience.
runners.comment: Point winner, tailed off in a bumper at Listowel last week, no hurdling experience

runners.name: Commodity Broker
runners.spotlight: Plenty went wrong on debut at Ballinrobe, since he was hampered early and raced a bit keenly, as well as showing a tendency to hang; not disgraced in the circumstances; may improve.
runners.comment: Plenty went wrong on debut at Ballinrobe, not disgraced in the circumstances, may improve

runners.name: Goldenkap
runners.spotlight: A E90,000 Goffs Arkle purchase as a 3yo; tenth foal; half-brother by Kapgarde to six winners, notably Gold Tweet, winner of 11 races over jumps in France; dam 1m2f winner.
runners.comment: E90,000 Goffs Arkle purchase as a 3yo, half-brother to a prolific French jumps winner

runners.name: Humble Berkshire
runners.spotlight: 66-1 chance when pulled up first time out at Limerick; safe to rule out.
runners.comment: 66-1 chance when pulled up first time out at Limerick, safe to rule out

runners.name: Mc Swagger
runners.spotlight: Made the frame in two bumper attempts; has yet to reach that standard over hurdles; dropped away tamely at Listowel last time.
runners.comment: Made the frame in two bumper attempts, has yet to reach that standard over hurdles

runners.name: Koliath De Couely
runners.spotlight: Beaten a short head a year ago on his only start for the Viel partnership in France; cost E150,000 last July; obvious chance in a race in which not many appeal.
runners.comment: Beaten a short head on his only start in France, cost E150,000 last July, obvious chance

runners.name: Sampoet
runners.spotlight: Is 1-16 in points; placed in hunter chases; not disgraced on recent hurdles debut but likely to need a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Poor strike rate in points, placed in hunter chases, not disgraced on recent hurdles debut

runners.name: Eastwestconnection
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in a point; well held by Sampoet on running in a maiden hurdle at Ballinrobe.
runners.comment: Pulled up in a point, well held by Sampoet on running in a maiden hurdle at Ballinrobe

runners.name: Let's Go La Fichad
runners.spotlight: Too keen and tailed off in both bumpers; improved when hooded on hurdles debut at Wexford, although again somewhat headstrong; failed to build on that at Tramore, faded after a bad blunder three out.
runners.comment: Fair third on hurdling debut at Wexford, failed to build on that at Tramore
runners.owner: Tinnahinch Construction Limited & Marie Therese Wall

runners.name: Alentejo
runners.spotlight: Modest form in three Flat maidens for William Haggas in 2024; never threatened at Ballinrobe on Irish/hurdling debut.
runners.comment: Modest Flat form in Britain, never threatened at Ballinrobe on Irish/hurdling debut

runners.name: Ballyminnion Boy
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete two point-to-point starts; remote tenth at Limerick on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Failed to complete two point-to-point starts, remote tenth at Limerick on hurdles debut

runners.name: Rosenborg
runners.spotlight: Placed in a point; plenty of improvement needed from his run at Punchestown last Saturday.
runners.comment: Placed in a point, plenty of improvement needed from his run at Punchestown last Saturday

races.race_name: Crawford Tiles And More Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Hurricane Honey
runners.spotlight: Went close over fences at this venue last October, seems better over longer distances; makes debut for this yard after a break.
runners.comment: Went close over fences at this venue last October, seems better over longer distances

runners.name: Soldier Saint
runners.spotlight: Placed in a bumper for Nicky Henderson; has shown improvement over longer distances in his two most recent races; return to shorter trip is a concern.
runners.comment: Has shown improvement over longer distances in his two most recent races, trip a concern

runners.name: Saint Peregrine
runners.spotlight: Lost form after finishing second in a maiden hurdle on this card a year ago; tailed off in a handicap hurdle here last August; has failed to take to chasing.
runners.comment: Lost form after finishing second at this venue a year ago, failed to take to chasing

runners.name: Moulane West
runners.spotlight: Locally-trained gelding won here early last season; overall record is unconvincing and getting on in years; down the field here four weeks ago.
runners.comment: Locally-trained gelding won here early last season, overall record is unconvincing

runners.name: Granlahan
runners.spotlight: Gave an improved display when stepped up to 2m4f at Limerick 12 days ago, promoted a place to third after the winner's disqualification; this trip may be a little shorter than ideal.
runners.comment: Gave an improved display when stepped up to 2m4f at Limerick, this trip may be inadequate

runners.name: Gforcejenny
runners.spotlight: Placed in a juvenile hurdle at last year's Listowel festival; satisfactory effort when fifth at Down Royal in her first handicap; a likely contender with normal improvement.
runners.comment: Satisfactory effort when fifth at Down Royal in her first handicap, a likely contender

runners.name: Annarubyrose
runners.spotlight: Third in a C&D maiden last year for Gavin Cromwell; fair recent form for this yard, but over longer distances than this at Downpatrick.
runners.comment: Third in a C&D maiden last year, fair recent form but over longer distances than this

runners.name: Theanniversary Man
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back winner last November/December at Thurles (2m, good to yielding) and Cork (2m4f, soft to heavy); pulled up early at Tramore ten days ago in a race in which Avandra produced a much-improved performance to take second; may bounce back.
runners.comment: Back-to-back winner last November/December, something amiss at Tramore, may bounce back

runners.name: Cholo
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise in two starts since returning from a break; has an edge over Avandra on Wexford running, but that rival showed improved form when second at Tramore recently.
runners.comment: Has shown promise in two starts since returning from a break, needs to find a bit extra

runners.name: Avandra
runners.spotlight: Was having her 18th start when placed for the first time ten days ago; never far from the pace in that 2m Tramore contest; may feature again, but not guaranteed to reverse previous Wexford form with Cholo.
runners.comment: Was having her 18th start when placed for the first time ten days ago, may feature again

runners.name: Sonny Fella
runners.spotlight: Never in the first four in 15 outings, has failed to complete three of his last four races.
runners.comment: Never in the first four in 15 outings, has failed to complete three of his last four races

runners.name: Timurshah
runners.spotlight: First reserve; weak form last year; a revelation at Clonmel last Friday on his first run for this stable; won with plenty to spare and would seem to have an excellent follow-up opportunity under a mandatory penalty if he gets a run.
runners.comment: First reserve, won with plenty to spare at Clonmel last Friday, strong contender

runners.name: Boston Bobby
runners.spotlight: Long-priced winner at Down Royal in December; may have needed Bellewstown run in April; trainer probably has a better chance with recent Tramore second Avandra.
runners.comment: Long-priced winner at Down Royal last December, may have needed Bellewstown run in April

runners.name: Mount Ferns
runners.spotlight: Hurdles win in 2024 came at Listowel (2m, soft) off 91; returned there last September for a staying-on third in the same event; pulled up at Cork a month ago on reappearance after a winter break.
runners.comment: Good run when third at Listowel last September, pulled up at Cork on reappearance
runners.owner: K McNamee & Manorplace Ltd & V Casey & F G Doran

runners.name: He Who Dares
runners.spotlight: Posed no threat when a 25-1 chance at Tramore ten days ago; safely held by Avandra on that running.
runners.comment: Posed no threat at Tramore ten days ago, safely held by Avandra on that running

runners.name: Coole Vision
runners.spotlight: Over four years since his last racecourse start, pulled up in a point-to-point in February.
runners.comment: Over four years since his last racecourse start, pulled up in a point-to-point in February

runners.name: Dark Berkshire
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; has shown little; only slight progress since switched to handicaps for his final two starts in 2025.
runners.comment: Second reserve, has shown little, only slight progress since switched to handicaps

runners.name: Grennanstown Lady
runners.spotlight: Showed little over hurdles last year; well beaten in two Flat starts this term; no fitness doubts, but hard to fancy.
runners.comment: Modest form in maiden hurdles, fit from two Flat outings but hard to fancy

runners.name: Glin Road Boy
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; weak form in maiden hurdles; market check advised if he gets a run on handicap debut and first run since his transfer to Charles Byrnes.
runners.comment: Third reserve, weak form in maiden hurdles, market check advised if he gets a run

races.race_name: Glasshouse Hotel (Q.R.) Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Aine's Lad
runners.spotlight: Won well over hurdles at Thurles in February and a fair chase debut next time at Fairyhouse, both on winter ground; however, has handled better ground in the past and run well here before too; chance.
runners.comment: Won at Thurles in February and a fair chase debut next time; has run well here; chance

runners.name: Smallcraftwarning
runners.spotlight: Has posted two hurdles and one chase win; his last seven runs have been over fences and some good efforts; has a shout back over hurdles.
runners.comment: Last seven runs over fences and some good efforts; has a shout back over hurdles

runners.name: Spellacy's Cross
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner is 0-17 over hurdles but has posted some solid runs lately at Punchestown and Limerick; should be involved again.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner is 0-17 over hurdles but solid runs lately and should be involved again
runners.owner: James M Barcoe & J F O'Neill

runners.name: Ilbdare
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D last year but has been hit and miss since (best run in that time was also here); has to be considered due to his record at this venue.
runners.comment: Won over C&D last year but has been hit and miss since; considered due to his record here

runners.name: Joshua's Daylami
runners.spotlight: Best form has come on heavy ground, winning a Punchestown maiden last year; was beaten 30l on return from a year away last time and has to improve lots from that.
runners.comment: Best form on heavy ground; beaten 30l on return from a year away last time

runners.name: Private Policy
runners.spotlight: Battled hard to win at Punchestown last time over 3m, his first score; up 6lb for today and should stay this trip; is lightly-raced for his age and likely still progressing; a player.
runners.comment: Battled hard to win at Punchestown last time over 3m; up 6lb and should stay trip; player

runners.name: Loyal Praetorian
runners.spotlight: Some fair runs in maidens including at Limerick last time but was pulled-up on handicap debut two starts ago at Cork; needs his best to figure.
runners.comment: Some fair runs in maidens but was pulled-up on h'cap debut two starts ago; needs his best

runners.name: Free Flowing
runners.spotlight: Maiden hurdle winner in 2023 but not at that level in two hurdles and one chase run since coming back after an absence.
runners.comment: Maiden hurdle winner in 2023 but not at that level back after an absence

runners.name: Je Suis
runners.spotlight: Has shown glimpses of potential over hurdles and fences; however, was pulled-up on return last time at Limerick and has to improve hugely from that.
runners.comment: Has shown glimpses of potential over hurdles and fences; pulled-up on return last time

runners.name: Lissen To The Lady
runners.spotlight: Six of her seven hurdle wins came in UK; has to find improvement on recent efforts here but not dismissed.
runners.comment: Six of seven hurdle wins came in UK; has to find improvement on recent efforts here

runners.name: Walking In Mayo
runners.spotlight: Point winner was badly hampered when favourite at Punchestown last time in a 3m handicap; clearly thought better than her form suggests; good ground probably helps; cheekpieces added and still of interest.
runners.comment: Badly hampered when fav' at Punchestown last time; cheekpieces added and still of interest

runners.name: Attaboyarchie
runners.spotlight: Looked set to win over fences last time at Limerick when the saddle slipped on the run-in, which would have been his first score; has a chance if as good over hurdles today.
runners.comment: Set to win over fences last time when the saddle slipped; chance if as good over hurdles

runners.name: Brandt
runners.spotlight: Dual chase winner has run well at times in defeat over hurdles but only three runs in this sphere in last two years; needs to do more.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner has run well at times over hurdles but needs to do more

runners.name: Jetstream Jeff
runners.spotlight: Pulled-up on handicap debut last time at Wexford and not easy to fancy from 5lb out of the handicap; tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Pulled-up on handicap debut last time and not easy to fancy; 5lb out of the handicap

runners.name: Eddies Pride
runners.spotlight: Reserve; dual chase winner is 0-22 over hurdles and 8lb out of the handicap so can't be fancied; a reserve in the 7.48 at Roscommon Monday.
runners.comment: Dual chase winner is 0-22 over hurdles and 8lb out of the handicap; reserve

runners.name: Sphagnum
runners.spotlight: Lost his form badly after winning the Cork National in 2024 and is hard to fancy on recent poor hurdle runs; blinkers replace cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Lost his form after winning the Cork National in 2024 and hard to fancy on recent runs

runners.name: The Dabbler
runners.spotlight: Veteran won over C&D in 2024; has been mostly over fences since during a truncated period and has to bring his best now back hurdling.
runners.comment: Won over C&D in 2024; mostly over fences since and has to bring his best back hurdling

races.race_name: Knocknarea Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Love Like This
runners.spotlight: A maiden after 31 hurdle starts but was a close second over C&D last month; has to put two lesser efforts behind her.
runners.comment: Close second over C&D last month; has to put two lesser efforts behind her

runners.name: Tradonthebay
runners.spotlight: Improved efforts in last two maiden hurdles since encountering good ground, including a second over C&D; considered on handicap debut as long as the ground doesn't deteriorate too much.
runners.comment: Better runs lately on good ground incl' over C&D; considered on handicap debut

runners.name: Itaintmebabe
runners.spotlight: Won a handicap at Fakenham last October over this trip but hasn't been near that level since including on debut for this yard; has to get back to best.
runners.comment: Won at Fakenham last October but hasn't been near that level since; has to return to best

runners.name: Phildante
runners.spotlight: Runner-up in a 3m handicap at Wexford last summer but hasn't been at that level since; has to improve from Downpatrick return.
runners.comment: Runner-up in a 3m handicap at Wexford last summer but hasn't been at that level since

runners.name: Larkins Lane
runners.spotlight: Narrowly got off the mark over this trip at Limerick last time on soft ground, battling on well; got a 6lb rise so will have to keep his forward momentum but a player if she does.
runners.comment: Up 6lb for Limerick win; will have to keep his forward momentum but a player if she does

runners.name: Clone Cross
runners.spotlight: Much-improved when runner-up, albeit beaten 15l, in a Wexford maiden hurdle last time; a 7lb rise for that makes it more difficult on handicap debut but can go well with a repeat.
runners.comment: Beaten 15l in a Wexford maiden hurdle last time; a 7lb rise makes this more difficult

runners.name: Thats All
runners.spotlight: Sole win over hurdles came at Kilbeggan last year and some decent runs in defeat since; however, was poor on return at Punchestown and tried to refuse then unseated rider last time at Limerick; risky.
runners.comment: Poor on return at Punchestown and tried to refuse then unseated rider last time; risky

runners.name: Joe's Turn
