runners.spotlight: First reserve; has a win apiece over hurdles and fences, both at Kilbeggan; beaten 30l on return at Punchestown but can improve from that and local yard is respected here.
runners.comment: Beaten 30l on return at Punchestown but can improve; local yard is respected here; reserve

runners.name: Joya Del Mar
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark over hurdles at the 20th attempt at Downpatrick over 2m6f last time; raised 10lb for that wide-margin score but a repeat of that effort can see her go close.
runners.comment: Raised 10lb for wide-margin score last time but a repeat of that can see her go close
runners.owner: Mrs T Giles-Fahey & Miss S Mangan

runners.name: Listentomewillya
runners.spotlight: Best maiden effort came on heavy ground at Limerick last winter; was poor on handicap debut at Clonmel last time.
runners.comment: Best maiden effort came on heavy ground last winter; poor on handicap debut at Clonmel

runners.name: Sarah's Express
runners.spotlight: Just hints of ability in maidens but has been poor in two handicaps; however, was favourite last time at Clonmel so is surely better than showing; can't be ruled out with blinkers added.
runners.comment: Beaten fav' last time at Clonmel so surely better than is showing; blinkers added

runners.name: Johnny M
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability in maidens in his early days; tailed-off on return from a long absence last time on stable debut; huge progress needed.
runners.comment: Tailed-off on return from a long absence last time on stable debut; huge progress needed

runners.name: Tantrum Boy
runners.spotlight: Second reserve; some improvement in first-time cheekpieces at Limerick last time but still needs plenty more.
runners.comment: Some improvement in cheekpieces at Limerick last time but still needs plenty more; reserve

runners.name: Max Time
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in a 2m6f handicap at Galway in 2024; basically regressive since and can't make a case for on recent form.
runners.comment: Basically regressive since late 2024 and can't make a case for on recent form

runners.name: Whatafoolbelieves
runners.spotlight: Showed promise in one of two bumper starts; not much in maiden hurdles but was still in with a chance when unseating two out on handicap debut at Downpatrick last time; one to consider.
runners.comment: In with a chance when unseating two out on h'cap debut at Downpatrick; one to consider

runners.name: Lake Okeechobee
runners.spotlight: Beaten nearly 8l behind Larkins Lane at Limerick last time over this trip; the cheekpieces worn then are retained and she should get closer to that rival on 6lb better terms.
runners.comment: Beaten nearly 8l behind Larkins Lane at Limerick last time; get closer on 6lb better terms

runners.name: Brave Josee
runners.spotlight: Was noted staying on from way off the pace on last two starts including at this track; could do better if getting a more prominent ride.
runners.comment: Stayed on from way off the pace on last two starts incl' here; could do better

runners.name: Backinabit
runners.spotlight: Best handicap run when fourth at Roscommon last year but well below that level twice after; needs to return to best on seasonal/stable debut.
runners.comment: Best handicap run when fourth at Roscommon last year but well below that level twice after

runners.name: Steps In The Sand
runners.spotlight: Third reserve; dual hurdle winner including over C&D in 2024; won on the Flat at Galway last summer and not a bad Ballinrobe hurdle run after; could run well if getting in.
runners.comment: C&D winner ran well enough on last hurdle run; could run well if getting in; reserve

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Mares (Pro/Am) INH Flat Race

runners.name: Moyneard
runners.spotlight: Promising debut in 2024 and similar efforts in defeat in four bumpers since returning in February; this is less competitive than Ballinrobe last time but she still needs to find more to win.
runners.comment: This is less competitive than Ballinrobe last time but she still needs to find more to win

runners.name: Whizz Bizz
runners.spotlight: Similar efforts in defeat in bumpers at Cork and Wexford over 2m; the step up in trip today could help her to get closer.
runners.comment: Similar efforts in defeat in bumpers over 2m; the step up in trip today could help

runners.name: Pets Georgina
runners.spotlight: The best of two bumper runs came on second start at Kilbeggan when beaten 14l; showed improved form on second hurdle start at Kilbeggan last time over 3m; can run well but this trip looks on the sharp side.
runners.comment: Improved form in a Kilbeggan maiden hurdle last time over 3m; trip looks on the sharp side

runners.name: Circus Hill
runners.spotlight: Went closest in three bumper starts when runner-up over C&D last August; made some errors but a fair run on hurdles/seasonal debut back here last time; can improve from that and course form is a positive so is likely to go well.
runners.comment: Best run came here last year; can improve from hurdles return and likely to go well
runners.owner: Dermot J Kelly & E J Burns & Sandra C Kelly

runners.name: Marvele De La Mare
runners.spotlight: Pulled-up in two points this spring and isn't easy to make a case for on rules debut; tongue-tie and visor on.
runners.comment: Pulled-up in two points this spring and isn't easy to make a case for on rules debut

runners.name: Treacys Ella
runners.spotlight: Third foal; dam point winner, out of 2m4f hurdle/chase winning half-sister to 3m2f chase winner Siouxme; by a promising young sire and from a respected yard so needs a market check on debut.
runners.comment: By a promising young sire and from a respected yard so needs a market check on debut

runners.name: Clovelly Charm
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister to 3m hurdle/chase winner Lunar Contact; dam unraced sister to Champion Bumper and smart 2m2f-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Briar Hill; by a top-class sire and from a smart damline so is worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: By a top-class sire and from a smart damline so is worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Glenmore Graduate
runners.spotlight: Not much promise in three point starts so isn't easy to make a case for on bumper debut
runners.comment: Not much promise in three point starts so isn't easy to make a case for on bumper debut

runners.name: My Claire's Maiden
runners.spotlight: First foal; dam unraced half-sister to 5f Group 3 winner Urban Beat, out of 1m1f/9.4f winner; by a useful sire and unsold for E14,500 as a yearling; best watched on debut unless the market suggests otherwise.
runners.comment: Decent pedigree but best watched on debut unless the market suggests otherwise

runners.name: Tennessee Waltz
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; half-sister to two winners inc Game Colours (2m-3m hurdle); dam 7.4f-1m2f winner (RPR 67), half-sister to high-class 2m-3m6f hurdle/chase winner Paddy's Return; by a top-class sire and related to some useful sorts so is worth a market check on debut.
runners.comment: By a top-class sire and related to some useful sorts so is worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Leva Deva
runners.spotlight: Ran green when pulled-up in a maiden hurdle here on debut so isn't easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Ran green when pulled-up in a maiden hurdle here on debut so isn't easy to make a case for

runners.name: Tacograph
runners.spotlight: The stable second-string but ran a fine race on debut in the Goffs Defender Bumper at the Punchestown festival; that form hasn't been tested since but she is likely to have improved and a leading player.
runners.comment: Third in the Goffs Defender Bumper on debut; should have improved and a leading player

runners.name: Wild Eagle
runners.spotlight: Tailed-off in two bumpers, including over C&D on debut; not easy to make a case for her.
runners.comment: Tailed-off in two bumpers, including over C&D on debut; not easy to make a case for her

runners.name: Rosie Apple
runners.spotlight: Didn't show much promise in her two completed point starts in 2024 and not easy to make a case for on return; tongue-tie on.
runners.comment: Didn't show much promise in her two completed point starts in 2024; tongue-tie

races.race_name: Racing TV Amateur Jockeys' Handicap

runners.name: Lumenbourg
runners.spotlight: All three wins have been at 7f, although has plenty of good runs at 6f; good third to a subsequent winner at Redcar last time (6f, good); should go well.
runners.comment: All 3 wins have been over 7f, but plenty of good runs at 6f, including last time; chance
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: Andalprofit
runners.spotlight: Won for today's rider off a 2lb lower mark at Redcar last time (6f, good to firm); today's softer conditions are not sure to suit.
runners.comment: Won off 2lb lower for this rider at Redcar last time; softer going not sure to suit

runners.name: Groundsman
runners.spotlight: AW winner for Richard Fahey (7f); well beaten on stable debut after a break on turf at Lingfield last month (7f, good) but should come on for the outing; first run on soft ground.
runners.comment: AW winner for Richard Fahey; should be better for reappearance run; first outing on soft

runners.name: Jet Warrior
runners.spotlight: Comes into this in good form having won over 6f at Ayr (good to firm) and Ripon (good) last month; up 10lb (5lb higher than last time) and a contender, but softer ground a concern.
runners.comment: Has won his last two races; 10lb rise; a contender, although this softer ground a concern

runners.name: Amaysmont
runners.spotlight: Better on the AW, but better handicapped on turf; fair third at Southwell two runs back (7f), but modest efforts either side of that; this softer ground no problem.
runners.comment: Better on AW, but rated lower on turf; fair AW third two runs back; a possible on that run

runners.name: Bay Breeze
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D in May last year (good to soft); beaten 7l when third at Ripon last week (6f, good); these are his ideal conditions and should go well.
runners.comment: C&D winner; fair third last week; ideally suited by these conditions and should go well

races.race_name: Watch Racing TV Live EBF Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Eevee Star
runners.spotlight: Dam excelled on very soft ground when winning a French Listed race (5f) as 2yo; 15-2 for Ripon debut (6f, good) when soon well adrift but she did some good late work into third of eight; ought to progress and the drop in trip on today's stiff track shouldn't hold her back too much.
runners.comment: Soon well adrift on Ripon debut (6f) but good late work into third; should improve plenty

runners.name: Rock Steady Beat
runners.spotlight: 110,000gns yearling; dam's winning offspring include Happy Romance, a Group 3 winner as 2yo and 3yo; 6-1 for York debut (6f, good) and he showed up well for a long way in a stronger race than this; can improve; good claims dropped to 5f.
runners.comment: 110,000gns yearling; 6-1 for 6f York debut, showed up well for long way; good chance

runners.name: Sydney Grace
runners.spotlight: Never near the leaders on Yarmouth debut (5f, good; 16-1) but signs of ability even so, albeit she swished her tail when asked to improve; may well do better for yard that's 1-5 with 2yos here.
runners.comment: Never near leaders on Yarmouth debut (5f) but looks capable of doing better today

runners.name: Excessive
runners.spotlight: May foal; 15,000euros foal; eighth foal; Cotai Glory gelded half-brother to winners Dorian Gray (1m2f-1m4f AW; RPR 65) and Flosrose (Italian 1m 2yo); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (97); a late foal who may need more time; yard also runs Kaiulani Spirit.
runners.comment: Out of 6f AW winner as 2yo; late foal who may need time and likely to come on for the run

runners.name: Blakey Ridge
runners.spotlight: March foal; 105,000euros yearling; first foal; by Sioux Nation out of unraced half-sister to 6f Group 1 winner Emaraaty Ana, out of 1m4f Listed winner; yard's 2yos can go well first time but 1-29 with them here.
runners.comment: 105,000euros yearling; dam half-sister to Gr1 6f winner; yard's 2yos can go well on debut

runners.name: Kaiulani Spirit
runners.spotlight: March foal; £16,000 yearling; ninth foal; gelded son of Caturra out of mare who won on debut for another yard (5f; RPR 94) and produced winners including Flyin Hawaiian (10.6f AW; RPR 82) and Oahu (6f AW 2yo; 77); market can guide regarding this one and stablemate Excessive.
runners.comment: £16,000 yearling; yard runs two and market can offer some clues

runners.name: Mangham
runners.spotlight: March foal; by Harry Angel; tenth foal; half-brother to winners Akeed Dubawi (1m AW; RPR 80), Tarnhelm (6f-1m; 72) and Billy McGarry (7f-8.6f, mostly AW; 66); dam 5f 2yo winner (90); has been gelded; jumps trainer combination's first 2yo runner; likely other days.
runners.comment: Out of 5f winner as 2yo; trainer combination's first 2yo runner; likely other days

runners.name: Yorkshire Dream
runners.spotlight: 38,000gns breeze-up 2yo; 9-1 for recent 5f debut at Redcar where he was never involved after starting slowly but hinted at some ability despite running green; should improve, perhaps when handicapping.
runners.comment: 38,000gns breeze-up 2yo; always behind on debut but signs of ability despite running green

races.race_name: Follow Racing TV On X Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Glory Road
runners.spotlight: Runner-up at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) last October on sole 2yo start and again showed considerable promise when third at Newbury (1m2f, good) last month; he's the one to beat on form.
runners.comment: Placed on both starts and he's the one to beat on form

runners.name: Affettuoso
runners.spotlight: Improved from his debut run at Doncaster (1m, soft) when runner-up at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) last December at 5-6; that latest form reads well and there can be optimism that this well-bred 3yo can continue to progress for his respected Newmarket trainer; key player.
runners.comment: Runner-up at Wolverhampton last December on second start and he's a key player

runners.name: Ceinture d'Orion
runners.spotlight: 9-2 when fourth of seven, beaten just over 5l, on debut in warm novice at Wetherby (1m2f, good to firm) in April; well beaten at Haydock (1m2f, soft) since and has something to prove on slow ground, but she's an each-way contender if rediscovering her initial promise.
runners.comment: Fourth in warm Wetherby novice on debut; well beaten since; e-w claims on initial promise

runners.name: Pepsea
runners.spotlight: Promising second when 16-1 on debut at Thirsk (1m, good) in April; perhaps unsuited by the fast ground (hung right) when 8l fourth of seven at Hamilton (1m1f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; holds each-way claims.
runners.comment: Promising second on debut at Thirsk and perhaps unsuited by the fast ground at Hamilton

runners.name: Sun Lord
runners.spotlight: Has shown ability on both starts (1m3f/1m2f, good to soft/good to firm) and he could have more to offer, but a sizeable step forward is needed.
runners.comment: Has shown ability on both starts but a sizeable step forward is needed

runners.name: Turret
runners.spotlight: 17-2, ran green when beaten a long way on last month's debut at Lingfield (1m, AW); a dramatic transformation is needed but he cost 580,000euros in 2024 and his dam is from an excellent family; should be capable of considerably better at some point.
runners.comment: Green when beaten a long way on debut; should be capable of lots better at some point

runners.name: Steel Raven
runners.spotlight: £52,000 yearling, 87,000euros 2yo breeze-ups; second foal; half-brother to 7f winner We've Got This (RPR 69); unraced dam from useful family; the betting could be informative on debut.
runners.comment: 87,000euros 2yo breeze-ups; the betting could be informative on debut

runners.name: Snake Hips
runners.spotlight: 25,000gns foal; twelfth foal; half-brother to five winners including Staxton (6f; RPR 106; for this yard), Lazarus Bell (7f 2yo; 89), Classic Pursuit (prolific 5f/6f; 89) and Satin Snake (7f AW; 81); dam 1m4f winner (80); likely to improve for this debut run but could give a good account.
runners.comment: Half-brother to five winners; likely to improve for this debut but could give good account

runners.name: Sodeto
runners.spotlight: 4yo who was tailed off at 40-1 in a bumper at Haydock (1m7f, good) on last month's debut; a tongue-tie replaces a hood; probably best watched.
runners.comment: 4yo who was tailed off at 40-1 in a bumper at Haydock (1m7f, good) on last month's debut

runners.name: Lorton Valley
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in fifth earlier this year on his first two starts (1m, AW) and he has plenty to find if he's to be competitive today.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in fifth earlier this year on his first two starts (1m, AW)

runners.name: Sanny Doo
runners.spotlight: He's achieved very little across his three starts (6f/5f).
runners.comment: He's achieved very little across his three starts (6f/5f)

runners.name: Thistle Nil
runners.spotlight: 4yo who was 66-1 when always behind and well beaten on last month's debut at Ayr (6f, good to firm); likely he's one for further down the line.
runners.comment: 4yo who was 66-1 when always behind and well beaten on last month's debut at Ayr

runners.name: Vintage Marsala
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has some potential in his pedigree but he was always in last place when 12-1 for his debut at Redcar (1m, good to firm) last August; needs to be an entirely different proposition on his reappearance.
runners.comment: Always in last place on his debut at Redcar last August; this 4yo makes his reappearance

races.race_name: racingtv.com Handicap

runners.name: Station X
runners.spotlight: All his wins have been at 6f, the latest at Southwell in April, but effective at 5f; goes particularly well on soft/heavy ground so today's conditions should be ideal; fair run last time at Hamilton (1.25l sixth of 13); could go well from his high draw.
runners.comment: All wins at 6f, but effective at 5f; well suited by soft/heavy and one with a chance

runners.name: Trilby
runners.spotlight: Had his ideal conditions when gaining his fourth win at Haydock last month (5f, soft) when wearing cheekpieces for the first time; solid third of 20 off this 5lb higher mark at York last time (5f, good; fifth has won since); stall 2 perhaps not ideal but one with a chance.
runners.comment: These conditions ideal; comes here in fine form so should go well but stall 2 not ideal

runners.name: Hundred Caps
runners.spotlight: Suited by 5f on a stiff track (two of his three wins have been at Beverley); fair fifth at Beverley with a visor tried last time (5f, good; pulled hard); regular cheekpieces back on here but soft ground a negative.
runners.comment: Suited by 5f on a stiff track; fair run on good last time; today's softer going not ideal

runners.name: Peter The Wolf
runners.spotlight: Only win was a 6f Chepstow maiden last September (6f, soft; 8-15 favourite); well beaten both starts since; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Only win was 6f maiden last September; well beaten both runs since; bit to prove

runners.name: Qila
runners.spotlight: Unexposed handicap debutant; best run when second in a 5f Newcastle novice last time; could go well but unproven on soft ground and this looks competitive.
runners.comment: Unexposed; good 2nd on Tapeta latest; interesting on h'cap debut but this is competitive

runners.name: Arctic Summer
runners.spotlight: Ex-French; only win was over 6f at Chantilly as a juvenile (very soft); not been in top form this season and needs cheekpieces to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Ex-French (won over 6f at Chantilly in 2024); going suits; not in top form; headgear tried

runners.name: Parisiac
runners.spotlight: Has won ten of his 50 races on the Flat, all over 5f, the latest on his reappearance at Hamilton (good) last week; goes well on soft/heavy ground; another good run on the cards.
runners.comment: 10-50, latest win at Hamilton last week; best at 5f, goes well on soft/heavy; chance

runners.name: Vince L'Amour
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been at his best since winning at Ripon early last month (5f, good); today's conditions ideal and could bounce back from good draw.
runners.comment: Not at his best since Ripon win in May; goes well on soft ground so could bounce back

runners.name: Spoof
runners.spotlight: Veteran; all 12 wins have been over 5f and is ideally suited by plenty of give in the ground; hasn't been out since Doncaster win in March (5f, good to soft); has plenty in his favour and should go well again.
runners.comment: All 12 wins at 5f and well suited by soft/heavy; back after two months off; should go well

runners.name: Francisco's Piece
runners.spotlight: Two wins over 5f on soft/heavy as a juvenile, the second a Listed event at Chantilly; hasn't won since and only seventh of nine on reappearance, but on a fair mark; could go well though stall 1 a slight negative.
runners.comment: Two wins over 5f on soft/heavy as a 2yo; not won since but conditions suit and a possible

runners.name: The Bell Conductor
runners.spotlight: Veteran; goes well on soft ground and ran a fine race when second to Trilby (2lb worse off for 0.5l) at Haydock last time (5f, soft; tongue-tie tried); tongue-tie retained and sound each-way chance.
runners.comment: Veteran; goes well on soft ground and has a sound each-way chance after good 2nd latest

races.race_name: Join Racing TV Now Handicap

runners.name: Jaminoz
runners.spotlight: Six-time winner, including over C&D last July (good to soft); well suited by give in the ground, so after two fair runs this season, is one with a chance.
runners.comment: Six-times winner, including over C&D; goes well on soft; two fair runs this term; chance

runners.name: Dubai Venture
runners.spotlight: Won twice over 1m4f on good to firm last season; began the season with a good second at Pontefract last month (1m4f, good to soft) and could go well if coping with this softer ground.
runners.comment: Two wins over 1m4f on good to firm in 2025; good second on reappearance; a possible

runners.name: Dr Rio
runners.spotlight: All six wins in Britain have been at Beverley, the latest over 7.5f last August (good); has also won at up to 1m4f there so has a chance following good run last time; soft ground a slight concern.
runners.comment: 6 British wins have been from 7.5f-1m4f at Beverley; fair 3rd latest; soft going a concern

runners.name: Golspie
runners.spotlight: Three wins have been at 1m/1m2f, one on soft ground; three fair runs in March (all over 1m2f, two on AW, the other on good to soft); hampered when a well-beaten third last time; could go well if as effective on softer ground and over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Better than his last run and could go well if as effective at 1m3f and on softer going

runners.name: V Twelve
runners.spotlight: Versatile; soundly beaten on both starts this year (1m4f Catterick handicap, 1m7f Hexham handicap chase); needs to bounce back, but has a chance on his form on the Flat last year; soft going not a problem.
runners.comment: Not been at his best two starts this year (over fences last time); chance on 2025 form

runners.name: Naturalia
runners.spotlight: Not the best of strike-rates but two sound runs this season, latest when runner-up over C&D 11 days (good); could go well but not particularly consistent; softer ground no problem.
runners.comment: Two sound runs this term, latest over C&D 11 days ago; could go well, but inconsistent

runners.name: Taylormade Lad
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last August (good to firm) who was subsequently runner-up at Hamilton (1m4f, soft); two fair runs this time, latest over C&D; chance.
runners.comment: C&D winner last August; two fair runs this term, acts on soft and one with a chance

runners.name: Letmeseethecolts
runners.spotlight: Won twice last year, second time over 1m4f at Pontefract in April (good); well beaten after 380-day break last time and has a bit to prove.
runners.comment: Two wins last year but well beaten after 380-day break last time and has a bit to prove

runners.name: West Tyrone
runners.spotlight: Ex-Kevin Ryan; won a 1m AW handicap in March 2025; stays 1m2f but below-par when tried over further; bit to prove.
runners.comment: Ex-Kevin Ryan; 1m AW winner; stays 1m2f (below-par when tried over further); bit to prove

runners.name: Polygram
runners.spotlight: Maiden; some fair runs on the Flat this year, notably when third at Ripon last time (1m4f, good); first run on soft ground.
runners.comment: Maiden; fair form on the Flat in 2026, notably when third last time; first run on soft

runners.name: Scotland The Brave
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle in February last year (2m); back after a wind op but probably best watched after 405-day break.
runners.comment: Won over 2m on AW in February 2025; back after 405-day break having had wind op

runners.name: Junkyard Dog
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish; fair hurdler; only Flat win was over 1m2f at Navan in April 2025 (good); handles soft ground; not out of it.
runners.comment: Ex-Irish; fair form over hurdles for new yard and not without a chance back on the Flat
runners.owner: A Sinclair, David Carroll & G Gibson

races.race_name: Racing TV Handicap

runners.name: Hatamoto
runners.spotlight: Runner-up on handicap debut here (1m, soft) last month and the drop back to 7f looked to be against him at Leicester (good to firm) eight days later, when he stayed on for third; high on the list.
runners.comment: Runner-up over 1m here last month and the drop back to 7f was against him eight days later

runners.name: Espanita
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in March on second start; she remains lightly raced and is not written off but she's failed to progress since that win and was only eighth of 12 on last month's turf debut (8.3f, good to soft).
runners.comment: Won at Wolverhampton in March but hasn't progressed since, most recently her turf debut

runners.name: Cosmic Man
runners.spotlight: Best 2yo run came when keeping on for close fourth here (6f, good) in September; however, he's struggled elsewhere on both runs this season (7.4f/7f, good to soft/AW) and a dramatic revival is needed back at Carlisle.
runners.comment: Best 2yo run came here but he's struggled elsewhere on both runs this season

runners.name: Attention Seeker
runners.spotlight: 0-9 and had been regressive this year, but last month's fourth of nine at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) was a step back in the right direction; ran well on soft ground last September and he's not ruled out.
runners.comment: 0-9 but step back in right direction last month and he's not ruled out

runners.name: Ibn Altheeb
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) last October in first-time cheekpieces and he's one to watch in the betting in first-time blinkers today, but he's struggled on both runs this season and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: One to watch in the betting in first-time blinkers but a major turnaround is necessary

runners.name: Clocker
runners.spotlight: She's raced three times and she showed up well before fading into fourth on her reappearance in a maiden at Newcastle (1m, AW) in April; her pedigree indicates this extra furlong will be a positive on her handicap debut and she's one to consider.
runners.comment: Pedigree indicates this extra furlong will be a positive on h'cap debut; one to consider

runners.name: Lightening Lad
runners.spotlight: Kept on for encouraging second on debut at Newcastle (7f, AW) last November; yet to build on that on his two runs since (1m/7f, AW/good) but his pedigree suggests the extra yardage could be a plus on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Promise on the first of his three starts; the extra yardage could be a plus on h'cap debut

runners.name: Ephron
runners.spotlight: Having been gelded he made a winning handicap and seasonal debut at Redcar (1m, good to firm) in April; only fifth at Haydock (1m2f, good to soft) since but faded late on and he could be in the mix over a furlong shorter.
runners.comment: Won over 1m in April on handicap debut and weakened when fifth over 1m2f subsequently
runners.owner: Middleham Park Racing Lxvi And P Demilow

runners.name: Scheffler
runners.spotlight: Promising third on debut at Lingfield (7f, AW) in March before two lesser runs (6f/1m, AW); has since left Richard Hughes; interesting to see how he goes in the betting in first-time cheekpieces on handicap/stable debut.
runners.comment: Promising debut before two lesser runs; h'cap/stable debut; interesting to note betting

runners.name: Unchartedterritory
runners.spotlight: 0-5 and hasn't progressed but ran respectably when third of six in Nottingham handicap (1m2f, good) last Wednesday; has run well on good to soft (unraced on softer); could have a part to play.
runners.comment: Respectable third at Nottingham last Wednesday and he could have a part to play

runners.name: Mr Moonshine
runners.spotlight: Kept on for neck second on nursery debut at Thirsk (1m, good) last September; soundly beaten at Beverley (8.4f, soft) 11 days later and he's unproven on slow ground, but he reappears having been gelded and remains one to be interested in given the Thirsk effort.
runners.comment: Went close on the first of two nursery starts last September; reappears having been gelded

races.race_name: Bet Now With fairplaybet.co.uk Apprentice Classified Stakes

runners.name: Banksman
runners.spotlight: 2-2 at this level with both wins coming for Jack Channon around this time last year; has not set the world alight in eight starts under both codes since joining this yard but he didn't run too badly in a 0-55 handicap here (1m, good to firm) 11 days ago; interesting back in this company.
runners.comment: 2-2 at this level and didn't run too badly last time; interesting back in this company

runners.name: Fai Fai
runners.spotlight: Not shown much in two starts on the AW since returning in March and just 3-50, but he was placed in all four starts in handicap company here (6f) last year; just about stays this far; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Placed in all four starts in handicap company here last year; shortlisted

runners.name: Barnsnape Boy
runners.spotlight: Won two 0-55 handicaps at about 5f (good) last August after the cheekpieces went on, including one here; the return to this grade is a positive but he hasn't got home in two previous attempts over C&D and the headgear is still missing.
runners.comment: Return to this grade is a positive but he hasn't got home in two previous C&D attempts

runners.name: Horus
runners.spotlight: 1-21; mainly seen on the AW but appears to handle turf; drops to this level for the first time and the stable won this last year; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Drops to this level for the first time and the stable won this last year; each-way claims

runners.name: Mokata
runners.spotlight: Out the back in three starts over 1m/1m2f during the spring; this is easier and a tongue-tie is applied but the market may be the best indication of whether a better performance is expected.
runners.comment: Modest in three starts during the spring; market useful down in grade; tongue-tie on

runners.name: Embarked
runners.spotlight: Often a front-runner; returns to turf for the first time since winning a 0-60 handicap at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last July but he does appear to face a difficult task on these terms.
runners.comment: On turf for the first time since winning at Doncaster last July but faces a difficult task

runners.name: Rainbow Sign
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 3-63; he won a 0-52 handicap here (1m, good to firm) last September and his latest fourth of 14 in a 0-55 handicap at Bath (1m, good) was commendable; frame possibilities again.
runners.comment: Has won here and latest fourth at Bath was commendable; frame possibilities

runners.name: Pacific Prince
runners.spotlight: Little to shout about in nine starts and the drop to this level has made no difference in his last two outings; best watched.
runners.comment: Little to shout about in nine starts and the drop to this level has made no difference

runners.name: Almavillalobas
runners.spotlight: Out of the frame in all ten starts from 5f-11.4f; makes little appeal.
runners.comment: Out of the frame in all ten starts from 5f-11.4f; makes little appeal
runners.owner: C Seeney, S Holden, Mildean , M Robinson

runners.name: Voodoo Ray
runners.spotlight: His hat-trick in 0-55 handicaps in the spring of 2024 included two wins here (6f/7f) but a lot of water has flowed under the bridge since then; hard to recommend on latest efforts, even in this company.
runners.comment: Four wins here but hard to recommend on latest efforts, even in this company

races.race_name: Download The Fairplay App Now Classified Stakes (Brighton Summer Sprint Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Mister Sandman
runners.spotlight: 0-11 but has made the frame in seven of his eight starts in handicap company, including when fourth of 12 in this headgear combination on his return from five months off at Bath (5.7f, good to firm) 18 days ago (She Went Whoosh second); drops to this level for the first time and respected.
runners.comment: 0-11, but has made the frame in seven of his eight starts in handicap company; respected

runners.name: Big Time Rascal
runners.spotlight: Just 1-23 albeit the win came in a 0-55 handicap here (6f, good) just over a year ago; runner-up in last two starts at Lingfield (6f, AW) and Bath (5.7f, good to firm) and it may be that a bit further and good or faster ground suits him best, but he still holds strong claims in this company.
runners.comment: Just 1-23 and 6f on good or faster ground may suit best, but he still holds strong claims

runners.name: Washington Heir
runners.spotlight: Not shown a great deal in six outings and needs to improve, even allowing for the drop in grade.
runners.comment: Not shown a great deal in six outings and needs to improve

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: Just 1-34 and her latest Wolverhampton effort (5f, AW) was modest, but she had previously run well under a positive ride when second of 12 in a 0-55 handicap at Bath (5.7f, good to firm); each-way claims if producing her best.
runners.comment: 1-34; second of 12 in a 0-55 handicap at Bath on penultimate start; each-way claims

runners.name: Poetic Jack
runners.spotlight: 2-35 with both wins coming on the AW; return to this trip shouldn't be a problem, but others are preferred for win purposes; cheekpieces return.
runners.comment: 2-35 with both wins coming on the AW; others are preferred for win purposes

runners.name: Lynwood Lad
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent and officially rated 4lb inferior to stablemate Mister Sandman, but his sole turf success came in this race last year when sent off 14-1; interesting with the cheekpieces back on.
runners.comment: Sole turf success came in this race last year; interesting with the cheekpieces back on

runners.name: Daley T
runners.spotlight: 0-23 and hasn't been shaping like a winner waiting to happen; cheekpieces will need to make a big difference; stable also runs Washington Heir.
runners.comment: 0-23 and hasn't been shaping like a winner waiting to happen; cheekpieces on

runners.name: Battle Point
runners.spotlight: Just 1-50 and his best efforts have come on the AW; stable appears to hold stronger claims with Big Time Rascal.
runners.comment: Just 1-50 and his best efforts have come on the AW; others preferred

races.race_name: Watch Live Racing On fairplaybet.co.uk 'Confined' Handicap (Horses With No Wins In 2026)

runners.name: Gone Rogue
runners.spotlight: Now 8lb higher than when successful over C&D (good to soft) in October; not at his best at Newbury (1m, good) last time, but his previous narrow defeat on his Windsor reappearance (1m2f, good to firm) suggested this sort of mark was within reach; shortlisted.
runners.comment: C&D winner last September; not at his best last time but only just beaten the time before

runners.name: Play Me
runners.spotlight: Still 5lb higher than for his sole success, at Epsom (8.5f, good) last August, but has made the frame in all eight outings since; that reliability counts for plenty in this field.
runners.comment: In the frame eight times since his sole success; that reliability counts for plenty
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Astronomica
runners.spotlight: Last win came in July 2023 and is still 2lb higher; off for 11 months but has won fresh before and she could be given an each-way chance if the ground is slower than good.
runners.comment: Has won fresh; could be given an each-way chance if the ground is slower than good

runners.name: Far And Above
runners.spotlight: Showed ability in his first two starts last year, but has regressed since; down another 3lb but he needs to find more for the return to turf.
runners.comment: Showed ability in his first two starts last year, but has regressed since

runners.name: Blue Collar Lad
runners.spotlight: Winner of seven races but still 5lb higher than when last successful 12 months ago and this trip looks right on the limit of his stamina.
runners.comment: 5lb higher than when last successful and this trip looks right on the limit of his stamina

runners.name: Lordsbridge Grey
runners.spotlight: Showed some ability last year, as when a close third of seven on handicap debut at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft) in September; gelded and underwent wind surgery prior to not justifying market support on last month's Southwell reappearance (1m, Tapeta); interesting to see if his backers return.
runners.comment: Didn't justify market support on his reappearance; interesting to see if backers return

runners.name: Tea Sea
runners.spotlight: Won here (7f, good to firm) last August and made an encouraging reappearance when fifth of 14 at Kempton (7f, AW) last month; does stay this far but could do with the ground drying out.
runners.comment: Won over 7f here last August; does stay this far, but could do with the ground drying out

runners.name: Rogue Dynasty
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on stable debut at Chester (7f, good; first-time hood) last July and has performed with credit in most of her outings since, although she finished over a length behind Amused at Lingfield (7f, good) eight days ago and meets him on the same terms.
runners.comment: Over a length behind Amused at Lingfield eight days ago and meets him on the same terms

runners.name: Tactical Plan
runners.spotlight: Not the most consistent but is well treated on his best form and drops into a 0-70 for the first time; worth a second look.
runners.comment: Not very consistent, but well treated on best form and drops into 0-70 for the first time

runners.name: King Of War
runners.spotlight: Three wins over 7f here included two last autumn; not seen in seven months but he's run well fresh before and has also performed well over this trip; well worth a market check.
runners.comment: Three wins over 7f here; has run well fresh and has also performed well over this trip

runners.name: Celestias Comet
runners.spotlight: Easily landed an uncompetitive fillies' novice at Southwell (1m, Tapeta; 4-11) last September, but hasn't built on it; return to this trip may suit but others are more compelling.
runners.comment: Won an uncompetitive fillies' novice at Southwell last September but hasn't built on it
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs Dowling And & Mrs Marney

runners.name: Amused
runners.spotlight: Again showed ability when second of eight at Lingfield (7f, good) eight days ago, shaping as though the return to this trip would suit; high on list from same mark; stable also runs Play Me.
runners.comment: Second at Lingfield eight days ago; return to 1m should suit; high on list off same mark
runners.owner: P And Mrs L Rowe And John Turner

runners.name: Gunfighter
runners.spotlight: Both wins on the AW (6f/7f); has run well on turf but looks best over shorter and finished in midfield on stable debut at Kempton (1m, AW) 13 days ago following a ten-month absence; best watched unless attracting support.
runners.comment: Midfield on stable debut at Kempton 13 days ago; best watched unless attracting support

races.race_name: Fairplay Lets Bet On It! Handicap

runners.name: Monks Mead
runners.spotlight: Only success in his last 28 starts came by a nose in a 0-50 classified event at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in January; finished behind a couple of these at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) last month; has a bit to find.
runners.comment: One win in his last 28 starts; behind a couple of these last time; has a bit to find

runners.name: Night Bear
runners.spotlight: Record here reads 3133, the win coming over 1m4f in June 2024; 0-21 since, though, and others are preferred for the win; stable triple-handed.
runners.comment: Record here reads 3133, but losing run up to 21; others are preferred for the win

runners.name: Bohemian Breeze
runners.spotlight: Disappointing favourite in first-time blinkers at Bath (11.6f, good) last month but that headgear is dispensed with and cheekpieces return; drops into a 0-55 for the first time and is 5lb lower than when winning over C&D (good to firm) last July; respected; stable also runs Letsbeatsepsis.
runners.comment: Drops into a 0-55 for the first time and 5lb lower than when winning over C&D last July

runners.name: Letsbeatsepsis
runners.spotlight: 0-13 but he ran well to finish third of 11 on his Lingfield reappearance (7.6f, good) last month and the return to this trip can only be a positive on the pick of last year's form; high on list.
runners.comment: 0-13 but the return to this trip can only be a positive on the pick of last year's form

runners.name: Celebrating Ethel
runners.spotlight: Last two wins have come in 0-50 classified events, the latest over C&D (good) last August; down another 3lb but others look more solid.
runners.comment: Last two wins have come in 0-50 classified events, the latest over C&D; others more solid

runners.name: Celtic John
runners.spotlight: Sole success in 17 starts came in a 0-50 classified event at Lingfield (1m, AW) in January; ran well when fourth of 11 here (1m, good to firm) 11 days ago but has still to convince over this far.
runners.comment: 1-17; fourth over 1m here 11 days ago but has still to convince over this far

runners.name: Foinix
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner over 1m4f; back off the same mark as for the latest last August but may find this trip sharp enough for him these days.
runners.comment: Dual course winner over 1m4f; back off last winning mark but may find this trip too sharp
runners.owner: M Robinson, Jack Pook & Jeanette May

runners.name: Fighting Poet
runners.spotlight: Ran well to finish fourth of eight on last month's Windsor return (1m2f, good to firm); all three wins have come here, the latest from 1lb lower over this trip last August; player.
runners.comment: Three wins have come here, the latest from 1lb lower over this trip last August; player

runners.name: Twilight Guest
runners.spotlight: Landed a hat-trick around this time last year, two of the wins coming over C&D including this race off 4lb higher; needs to improve plenty on recent efforts if he is to exploit this mark, though.
runners.comment: 4lb lower than when winning this race last year; needs to improve plenty on recent efforts

runners.name: Love You More
runners.spotlight: 0-11 and modest at Wolverhampton (8.6f, AW) last month but her best efforts have come on turf, as when second of ten on her Nottingham return (1m, good) in April; respected back on grass over a longer trip with plenty of stamina on the dam's side of her pedigree.
runners.comment: 0-11 but respected back on turf and up in trip, with stamina on dam's side of pedigree

runners.name: Masqool
runners.spotlight: Losing run up to 13 but has made the frame in two starts at Windsor (1m2f, good to firm) since returning to turf last month; each-way claims again with first-time blinkers given a go.
runners.comment: Losing run up to 13, but in the frame in his two recent starts at Windsor; blinkers on

runners.name: Power Of Prayer
runners.spotlight: Landed a Dundalk handicap (1m4f, AW) for Joseph O'Brien in November but finished out of the frame when sent off favourite for her next two starts back there; makes her stable debut after five months off having been picked up for 8,000gns in February; market useful.
runners.comment: Won at Dundalk in November; makes her stable debut after five months off; market useful

runners.name: Turpin
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in all eight starts over 7f-8.5f, his best efforts coming on the AW; nothing in his pedigree to suggest that stepping up in trip will prove the answer.
runners.comment: Unplaced in all eight starts; nothing to suggest that a longer trip will prove the answer

runners.name: Little Tiger
runners.spotlight: Last win came in emphatic fashion over C&D (good to soft) in October 2024; still 6lb higher and he may just need this after 221 days off.
runners.comment: Last win came over C&D in October 2024; still 6lb higher and he may just need this

races.race_name: Fairplay Daily Price Boosts Handicap

runners.name: Arvana Belle
runners.spotlight: Record in 6f/7f nurseries here last August/September reads 123, but modest in two starts since returning from seven months off in April; chance depends on the return to this venue doing the trick.
runners.comment: Record in nurseries here in 2025 reads 123; chance depends on return here doing the trick

runners.name: Cape Toronada
runners.spotlight: Has improved since switching to turf handicaps last month, finishing fourth of ten at Bath (5.7f, good) and second of 12 at Windsor (6f, good); up 1lb but still a major player.
runners.comment: Has improved since switching to turf handicaps last month; up 1lb but still a major player

runners.name: Saeculamation
runners.spotlight: Doesn't hold many secrets after 15 starts, but is 2lb lower than for his sole success at Chelmsford (6f, AW) in December and has performed well in all three starts since returned to turf, running even better than it looked when fourth of 13 from the worst draw at Lingfield (6f, good) a fortnight ago; high on list.
runners.comment: 2lb lower than for sole success and has run well in three starts since returned to turf

runners.name: A Lott Of Kane
runners.spotlight: Mainly running well since switched to AW handicaps including a win at Wolverhampton (6f) in March; faded into fifth at Leicester (5f) yesterday when returned to turf; needs to step up on that effort.
runners.comment: Running well since switched to AW handicaps; faded into fifth yesterday returned to turf

runners.name: No Claims Bonus
runners.spotlight: Twice placed in eight starts and by no means disgraced when fifth of 13 at Lingfield (6f, good) a fortnight ago, finishing half a length behind Saeculamation; each-way claims.
runners.comment: Twice placed in eight starts and not disgraced at Lingfield last time; each-way claims

runners.name: Poetic Grace
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame in all three starts since returning in handicap company with blinkers added in April, the latest when third of 12 behind a subsequent winner at Lingfield (7f, good) three weeks ago; this first attempt at sprinting poses the biggest question.
runners.comment: In the frame in all three handicaps; first attempt at sprinting poses the biggest question

runners.name: Rivera Queen
runners.spotlight: Finished a promising third of 12 on her Redcar debut (6f, good to firm) in May last year, but then went missing for 11 months and has been well held in both starts since returning; needs the switch to a handicap and new headgear combination to bring about a resurgence.
runners.comment: Needs the switch to a handicap and new headgear combination to bring about a resurgence
runners.owner: D Macauliffe, A Don & J Joyce

runners.name: Lieutenant Sir
runners.spotlight: Well held in three 5f AW novices late last year; makes his turf/handicap debut after six months off and the market should prove informative.
runners.comment: Makes his turf/handicap debut after six months off; market should prove informative

races.race_name: fairplaybet.co.uk Bet £20 Get £20 Free Bets Handicap

runners.name: Kento
runners.spotlight: Effective from the front as he showed when making all and winning by a nose over C&D (good to firm) 11 days ago; 4lb rise begs a question, though, and he is unraced on ground softer than good.
runners.comment: Made all over C&D 11 days ago; 4lb higher and unraced on ground softer than good

runners.name: Recon Mission
runners.spotlight: Now at the veteran stage; may have needed last month's Lingfield reappearance and is back off the same mark as when beaten a head over C&D last September; can make the running; stable also runs Kento.
runners.comment: Back off the same mark as when beaten a head over C&D last September; can make the running

runners.name: Merrimack
runners.spotlight: Handy sort whose record here reads 131; has run well in both starts under Chloe Lyons since returning to turf last month, going down by just a neck at Newbury (5f, good to firm) eight days ago; respected off the same mark.
runners.comment: Handy sort whose record here reads 131; beaten a neck at Newbury eight days ago; respected

runners.name: Secret Handsheikh
runners.spotlight: A bit in and out lately, but having dropped 5lb below his last winning mark he finished a close third of 13 here (6f, good to firm) 11 days ago; return to this trip will be no problem given his record over C&D reads 3121351123; high on list.
runners.comment: 5lb below last winning mark; record over C&D reads 3121351123; high on list

runners.name: Roman Spring
runners.spotlight: Dual course winner over 6f but he won over 5f on the Lingfield AW in November; becoming better handicapped but others are more appealing on recent efforts.
runners.comment: Dual course winner over 6f; becoming better handicapped but others still more appealing

runners.name: Ancient State
runners.spotlight: Has done well since joining Adam Kirby and his record over 5f reads 11122, but he finished out of the frame in his only start on turf which does pose a question.
runners.comment: Record over 5f reads 11122, but he finished out of the frame in his only start on turf

runners.name: Rory Rocket
runners.spotlight: Sole success in 12 starts came on the AW but he has run well on various surfaces on turf; has proved inconsistent since returning in February, though, and he can miss the break.
runners.comment: Has proved inconsistent since returning in February and can miss the break

runners.name: Mick's Spirit
runners.spotlight: Front-runner who is 11-61 on the AW, but 0-6 on turf; has a bit to prove and will probably need the ground to dry out in any case.
runners.comment: 0-6 on turf; has a bit to prove and will probably need the ground to dry out in any case
